text.Rotherham: Which of the prophets, did not your fathers persecute? Yea they slew them who declared beforehand concerning the coming of the Righteous One—of whom, now, ye, have become, betrayers and murderers!
text.KJV: 7:52 Which of the Prophets haue not your fathers persecuted? And they haue slaine them which shewed before of the comming of the Iust one, of whom ye haue bene now the betrayers and murderers:

text.NRSV: You are the ones that received the law as ordained by angels, and yet you have not kept it.”
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, received the law through ranks of messengers,—and guarded it not.
text.KJV: 7:53 Who haue receiued the Lawe by the disposition of Angels, and haue not kept it.

text.NRSV: When they heard these things, they became enraged and ground their teeth at Stephen.
text.Rotherham: And, while they were hearing these things, they were being pierced in their hearts, and began gnashing their teeth against him.
text.KJV: 7:54 ¶ When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.

text.NRSV: But filled with the Holy Spirit, he gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
text.Rotherham: But he, being already full of Holy Spirit, looking steadfastly into heaven, saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God;
text.KJV: 7:55 But hee being full of the holy Ghost, looked vp stedfastly into heauen, and saw the glory of God, and Iesus standing on the right hand of God,

text.NRSV: “Look,” he said, “I see the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”
text.Rotherham: And said—Lo! I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man, standing, on the right hand of God.
text.KJV: 7:56 And said, Behold, I see the heauens opened, and the Sonne of man standing on the right hand of God.

text.NRSV: But they covered their ears, and with a loud shout all rushed together against him.
text.Rotherham: And, crying out with a loud voice, they held their ears, and rushed with one accord upon him;
text.KJV: 7:57 Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their eares, and ran vpon him with one accord,

text.NRSV: Then they dragged him out of the city and began to stone him; and the witnesses laid their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text.Rotherham: And, thrusting him forth outside the city, proceeded to stone him. And, the witnesses, laid their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text.KJV: 7:58 And cast him out of the citie, and stoned him: and the witnesses layd downe their clothes at a yong mans feete, whose name was Saul.

text.NRSV: While they were stoning Stephen, he prayed, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”
text.Rotherham: And they stoned Stephen, as he was invoking and saying—Lord Jesus! give welcome unto my spirit.
text.KJV: 7:59 And they stoned Steuen, calling vpon God, and saying, Lord Iesus receiue my spirit.

text.NRSV: Then he knelt down and cried out in a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” When he had said this, he died.
text.Rotherham: And, kneeling down, he cried out with a loud voice—Lord! do not charge against them, this sin. And, having said this, he fell asleep.
text.KJV: 7:60 And he kneeled downe, and cried with a loud voice, Lord lay not this sinne to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleepe.

text.Base: And Saul approved of their killing him. That day a severe persecution began against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout the countryside of Judea and Samaria.
text.KJV: 8:1 And Saul was consenting vnto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the Church which was at Hierusalem, and they were all scattered abroad through out the regions of Iudea, and Samaria, except the Apostles.

text.Base: Devout men buried Stephen and made loud lamentation over him.
text.KJV: 8:2 And deuout men carried Steuen to his buriall, and made great lamentation ouer him.

text.Base: But Saul was ravaging the church by entering house after house; dragging off both men and women, he committed them to prison.
text.KJV: 8:3 As for Saul, he made hauocke of the Church, entring into euery house, and hailing men and women, committed them to prison.

text.Base: Now those who were scattered went from place to place, proclaiming the word.
text.KJV: 8:4 Therefore they that were scattered abroad, went euery where preaching the word.

text.Base: Philip went down to the city of Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah to them.
text.KJV: 8:5 Then Philip went downe to the citie of Samaria, and preached Christ vnto them.

text.Base: The crowds with one mind paid attention to what was said by Philip, when they heard him and saw the signs that he was doing.
text.KJV: 8:6 And the people with one accord gaue heed vnto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.

text.Base: For unclean spirits, crying with loud shrieks, came out of many who were possessed; and many others who were paralyzed or lame were cured.
text.KJV: 8:7 For vncleane spirits, crying with lowd voyce, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.

text.Base: So there was great joy in that city.
text.KJV: 8:8 And there was great ioy in that citie.

text.Base: Now a certain man named Simon had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, saying that he was someone great.
text.KJV: 8:9 But there was a certaine man called Simon, which before time in the same citie vsed sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giuing out that himselfe was some great one.

text.Base: All of them, from the least to the greatest, listened to him eagerly, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called Great.”
text.KJV: 8:10 To whom they all gaue heed from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.

text.Base: And they listened eagerly to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his magic.
text.KJV: 8:11 And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.

text.Base: But when they believed Philip, who was proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
text.KJV: 8:12 But when they beleeued Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdome of God, and the Name of Iesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

text.Base: Even Simon himself believed. After being baptized, he stayed close to Philip and was amazed when he saw the signs and great miracles that were taking place.
text.KJV: 8:13 Then Simon himselfe beleeued also: and when hee was baptized, hee continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signes which were done.

text.Base: Now when the apostles at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
text.KJV: 8:14 Now when the Apostles which were at Hierusalem, heard that Samaria had receiued the word of God, they sent vnto them Peter and Iohn.

text.Base: The two went down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit
text.KJV: 8:15 Who when they were come downe, praied for them that they might receiue the holy Ghost.

text.Base: (for as yet the Spirit had not come upon any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus).
text.KJV: 8:16 (For as yet hee was fallen vpon none of them: onely they were baptized in the Name of the Lord Iesus.)

text.Base: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 8:17 Then layde they their hands on them, and they receiued the holy Ghost.

text.Base: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,
text.KJV: 8:18 And when Simon saw that through laying on of the Apostles hands, the holy Ghost was giuen, hee offered them money,

text.Base: saying, “Give me also this power so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
text.KJV: 8:19 Saying, Giue me also this power, that on whomsoeuer I lay handes, hee may receiue the holy Ghost.

text.Base: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain God’s gift with money!
text.KJV: 8:20 But Peter said vnto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.

text.Base: You have no part or share in this, for your heart is not right before God.
text.KJV: 8:21 Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter, for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.

text.Base: Repent therefore of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, the intent of your heart may be forgiven you.
text.KJV: 8:22 Repent therefore of this thy wickednesse, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiuen thee.

text.Base: For I see that you are in the gall of bitterness and the chains of wickedness.”
text.KJV: 8:23 For I perceiue that thou art in the gall of bitternesse, and in the bond of iniquitie.

text.Base: Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that nothing of what you have said may happen to me.”
text.KJV: 8:24 Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for mee, that none of these things which ye haue spoken, come vpon me.

text.Base: Now after Peter and John had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, proclaiming the good news to many villages of the Samaritans.
text.KJV: 8:25 And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Hierusalem, and preached the Gospel in many villages of the Samaritanes.

text.Base: Then an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Get up and go toward the south to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” (This is a wilderness road.)
text.KJV: 8:26 And the Angel of the Lord spake vnto Philip, saying, Arise, and goe toward the South, vnto the way that goeth downe from Hierusalem vnto Gaza, which is desert.

text.Base: So he got up and went. Now there was an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of the Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, in charge of her entire treasury. He had come to Jerusalem to worship
text.KJV: 8:27 And hee arose, and went: and behold, a man of Ethiopia, an Eunuch of great authority vnder Candace queene of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Hierusalem for to worship,

text.Base: and was returning home; seated in his chariot, he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
text.KJV: 8:28 Was returning, and sitting in his charet, read Esaias the Prophet.

text.Base: Then the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over to this chariot and join it.”
text.KJV: 8:29 Then the Spirit saide vnto Philip, Goe neere, and ioyne thy selfe to this charet.

text.Base: So Philip ran up to it and heard him reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
text.KJV: 8:30 And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him reade the Prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?

text.Base: He replied, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to get in and sit beside him.
text.KJV: 8:31 And hee said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip, that hee would come vp, and sit with him.

text.Base: Now the passage of the scripture that he was reading was this: “Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter, and like a lamb silent before its shearer, so he does not open his mouth.
text.KJV: 8:32 The place of the Scripture, which hee read, was this, Hee was led as a sheepe to the slaughter, & like a Lambe dumbe before the shearer, so opened he not his mouth:

text.Base: In his humiliation justice was denied him. Who can describe his generation? For his life is taken from the earth.”
text.KJV: 8:33 In his humiliation, his Iudgement was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? For his life is taken from the earth.

text.Base: The eunuch asked Philip, “About whom, may I ask you, does the prophet say this, about himself or about someone else?”
text.KJV: 8:34 And the Eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the Prophet this? of himselfe, or of some other man?

text.Base: Then Philip began to speak, and starting with this scripture, he proclaimed to him the good news about Jesus.
text.KJV: 8:35 Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same Scripture, and preached vnto him Iesus.

text.Base: As they were going along the road, they came to some water; and the eunuch said, “Look, here is water! What is to prevent me from being baptized?”
text.KJV: 8:36 And as they went on their way, they came vnto a certaine water: and the Eunuch said, See, here is water, what doeth hinder me to be baptized?

text.Base: He commanded the chariot to stop, and both of them, Philip and the eunuch, went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
text.KJV: 8:38 And he commanded the charet to stand still: and they went downe both into the water, both Philip, and the Eunuch, and he baptized him.

text.Base: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away; the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
text.KJV: 8:39 And when they were come vp out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the Eunuch saw him no more: and hee went on his way reioycing.

text.Base: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he was passing through the region, he proclaimed the good news to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.
text.KJV: 8:40 But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing thorow he preached in all the cities, till he came to Cesarea.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile Saul, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest
text.Rotherham: But, Saul, yet breathing threatening and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, going unto the High-priest,
text.KJV: 9:1 And Saul yet breathing out threatnings & slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went vnto the high Priest,

text.NRSV: and asked him for letters to the synagogues at Damascus, so that if he found any who belonged to the Way, men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: asked from him letters for Damascus, unto the synagogues; to the end that, if he should find, any, who were of The Way, whether men or women, he might bring them, bound, unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 9:2 And desired of him letters to Damascus, to the Synagogues, that if hee found any of this way, whether they were men or women, hee might bring them bound vnto Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: Now as he was going along and approaching Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.
text.Rotherham: But, as he was journeying, it came to pass that he was drawing near unto Damascus, and, suddenly, there flashed around him a light out of heaven;
text.KJV: 9:3 And as he iourneyed he came neere Damascus, and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heauen.

text.NRSV: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?”
text.Rotherham: And, falling unto the earth, he heard a voice saying unto him—Saul! Saul! why, me, art thou persecuting?
text.KJV: 9:4 And he fel to the earth, and heard a voice saying vnto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

text.NRSV: He asked, “Who are you, Lord?” The reply came, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Who art thou, Lord? And, he, [said] —I, am Jesus, whom, thou, art persecuting!
text.KJV: 9:5 And he said, Who art thou Lord? And the Lord said, I am Iesus whom thou persecutest: It is hard for thee to kicke against the prickes.

text.NRSV: But get up and enter the city, and you will be told what you are to do.”
text.Rotherham: But rise up, and enter into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.
text.KJV: 9:6 And he trembling and astonished, said, Lord, what wilt thou haue mee to doe? And the Lord said vnto him, Arise, and goe into the citie, and it shall be told thee what thou must doe.

text.NRSV: The men who were traveling with him stood speechless because they heard the voice but saw no one.
text.Rotherham: But, the men who were accompanying him, stood speechless,—hearing, indeed, the voice, but beholding no one.
text.KJV: 9:7 And the men which iourneyed with him, stood speechlesse, hearing a voice, but seeing no man.

text.NRSV: Saul got up from the ground, and though his eyes were open, he could see nothing; so they led him by the hand and brought him into Damascus.
text.Rotherham: And Saul arose from the earth, and, his eyes being opened, he could see nothing; and, taking him by the hand, they led him into Damascus,—
text.KJV: 9:8 And Saul arose from the earth, and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.

text.NRSV: For three days he was without sight, and neither ate nor drank.
text.Rotherham: And he was three days without seeing, and did neither eat nor drink.
text.KJV: 9:9 And he was three dayes without sight, and neither did eate, nor drinke.

text.NRSV: Now there was a disciple in Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias.” He answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now there was a certain disciple in Damascus, by name Ananias; and, the Lord, said unto him in a vision—Ananias! And, he, said—Lo! I, [am here], Lord!
text.KJV: 9:10 ¶ And there was a certaine disciple at Damascus, named Ananias, and to him said the Lord in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the street called Straight, and at the house of Judas look for a man of Tarsus named Saul. At this moment he is praying,
text.Rotherham: And the Lord [said] unto him—Rise! go into the street which is called Straight, and seek, in the house of Judas, one Saul, by name, of Tarsus. For lo! he is praying,—
text.KJV: 9:11 And the Lord said vnto him, Arise, and goe into the street, which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Iudas, for one called Saul of Tarsus: for behold, he prayeth,

text.NRSV: and he has seen in a vision a man named Ananias come in and lay his hands on him so that he might regain his sight.”
text.Rotherham: And hath seen a man [in a vision], Ananias by name, coming in and laying on him his hands, to the intent he should see.
text.KJV: 9:12 And hath seene in a vision a man named Ananias, comming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receiue his sight.

text.NRSV: But Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he has done to your saints in Jerusalem;
text.Rotherham: And Ananias, answered—Lord! I have heard from many concerning this man, how many evil things unto thy saints he hath done in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: 9:13 Then Ananias answered, Lord, I haue heard by many of this man, how much euill hee hath done to thy Saints at Hierusalem:

text.NRSV: and here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who invoke your name.”
text.Rotherham: And, here, he hath authority from the High-priests to bind all them that call upon thy name.
text.KJV: 9:14 And here he hath authoritic from the chiefe Priests, to binde all that call on thy Name.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to him, “Go, for he is an instrument whom I have chosen to bring my name before Gentiles and kings and before the people of Israel;
text.Rotherham: But the Lord said unto him—Be going thy way; for, a choice vessel unto me, is this man, to bear my name before both [the] nations and kings, and the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 9:15 But the Lord said vnto him, Goe thy way: for hee is a chosen vessell vnto me, to beare my Name before the Gentiles, and Kings, and the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: I myself will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.”
text.Rotherham: For, I, will let him understand how many things he must needs, for my name, suffer.
text.KJV: 9:16 For I will shew him how great things hee must suffer for my Names sake.

text.NRSV: So Ananias went and entered the house. He laid his hands on Saul and said, “Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on your way here, has sent me so that you may regain your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: And Ananias departed, and entered into the house; and, laying upon him his hands, said—Saul, brother! The Lord, hath sent me,—Jesus, who appeared unto thee in the way by which thou wast coming,—that thou mayest recover sight, and be filled with Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 9:17 And Ananias went his way, and entred into the house, and putting his hands on him, said, Brother Saul, the Lord (euen Iesus that appeared vnto thee in the way as thou camest) hath sent me, that thou mightest receiue thy sight, and be filled with the holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: And immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and his sight was restored. Then he got up and was baptized,
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, there fell from him—from his eyes,—as it were scales; he recovered sight also, and, arising, was immersed:
text.KJV: 9:18 And immediatly there fell from his eyes as it had bene scales, and he receiued sight forthwith, and arose, and was baptized.

text.NRSV: and after taking some food, he regained his strength. For several days he was with the disciples in Damascus,
text.Rotherham: And, receiving food, gained strength. And he came to be with the disciples who were in Damascus certain days;
text.KJV: 9:19 And when hee had receiued meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certaine dayes with the disciples which were at Damascus.

text.NRSV: and immediately he began to proclaim Jesus in the synagogues, saying, “He is the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, in the synagogues, he began proclaiming Jesus, that, This, is the Son of God.
text.KJV: 9:20 And straightway hee preached Christ in the Synagogues, that hee is the Sonne of God.

text.NRSV: All who heard him were amazed and said, “Is not this the man who made havoc in Jerusalem among those who invoked this name? And has he not come here for the purpose of bringing them bound before the chief priests?”
text.Rotherham: And all who were hearing were astonished, and began to say—is not, this, he who destroyed, in Jerusalem, them that invoke this name; and, here, for this purpose, had come, in order that he might lead them, bound, unto the High-priests?
text.KJV: 9:21 But all that heard him, were amazed, and said, Is not this he that destroyed them which called on this Name in Hierusalem, and came hither for that intent that he might bring them bound vnto the chiefe Priests?

text.NRSV: Saul became increasingly more powerful and confounded the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus was the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: But, Saul, was the more gaining power; and was confounding the Jews who dwelt in Damascus, shewing, by comparison, that—This, is the Christ.
text.KJV: 9:22 But Saul increased the more in strength, and confounded the Iewes which dwelt at Damascus, proouing that this is very Christ.

text.NRSV: After some time had passed, the Jews plotted to kill him,
text.Rotherham: Now, when a considerable number of days were being fulfilled, the Jews took counsel together to kill him;
text.KJV: 9:23 ¶ And after that many dayes were fulfilled, the Iewes tooke counsel to kill him.

text.NRSV: but their plot became known to Saul. They were watching the gates day and night so that they might kill him;
text.Rotherham: But their plot was made known unto Saul,—and they were even narrowly watching the gates, both day and night, that they might kill him;
text.KJV: 9:24 But their laying awaite was knowen of Saul: and they watched the gates day and night to kill him.

text.NRSV: but his disciples took him by night and let him down through an opening in the wall, lowering him in a basket.
text.Rotherham: But the disciples, taking him by night, through the wall, let him down, lowering him in a basket.
text.KJV: 9:25 Then the disciples tooke him by night, and let him downe by the wall in a basket.

text.NRSV: When he had come to Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples; and they were all afraid of him, for they did not believe that he was a disciple.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had arrived in Jerusalem, he made attempts to join himself unto the disciples; and all were afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.
text.KJV: 9:26 And when Saul was come to Hierusalem, he assayed to ioyne himselfe to the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, and beleeued not that he was a disciple.

text.NRSV: But Barnabas took him, brought him to the apostles, and described for them how on the road he had seen the Lord, who had spoken to him, and how at Damascus he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: But, Barnabas, taking him, brought him unto the apostles, and related unto them,—how, in the way, he had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken unto him; and how, in Damascus, he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.
text.KJV: 9:27 But Barnabas tooke him, and brought him to the Apostles, and declared vnto them how hee had seene the Lord in the way, and that hee had spoken to him, and how hee had preached boldly at Damascus in the Name of Iesus.

text.NRSV: So he went in and out among them in Jerusalem, speaking boldly in the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he was with them, coming in and going out in Jerusalem, speaking boldly in the name of the Lord;
text.KJV: 9:28 And he was with them comming in, and going out at Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: He spoke and argued with the Hellenists; but they were attempting to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And was both speaking and discussing with the Grecian Jews,—but, they, were setting to work to kill him.
text.KJV: 9:29 And he spake boldly in the Name of the Lord Iesus, and disputed against the Grecians: but they went about to slay him.

text.NRSV: When the believers learned of it, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
text.Rotherham: And the brethren, discovering it, brought him down into Caesarea, and sent him away unto Tarsus.
text.KJV: 9:30 Which when the brethren knewe, they brought him downe to Cesarea, and sent him foorth to Tarsus.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace and was built up. Living in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, it increased in numbers.
text.Rotherham: So then, the assembly throughout the whole of Judaea and Galilee and Samaria, had peace, building itself up, and going on its way in the fear of the Lord; and, by the advocacy of the Holy Spirit, was being multiplied.
text.KJV: 9:31 Then had the Churches rest thorowout all Iudea, and Galilee, and Samaria, and were edified, and walking in the feare of the Lord, and in the comfort of the holy Ghost, were multiplied.

text.NRSV: Now as Peter went here and there among all the believers, he came down also to the saints living in Lydda.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Peter, going through all [quarters] went down unto the saints also dwelling in Lydda.
text.KJV: 9:32 ¶ And it came to passe, as Peter passed thorowout all quarters, he came downe also to the Saints, which dwelt at Lydda.

text.NRSV: There he found a man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years, for he was paralyzed.
text.Rotherham: And he found there a certain man, by name Aeneas, who, for eight years, had been lying prostrate upon a bed, for he was paralysed.
text.KJV: 9:33 And there he found a certaine man named Aeneas, which had kept his bed eight yeeres, and was sicke of the palsie.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; get up and make your bed!” And immediately he got up.
text.Rotherham: And Peter said unto him—Aeneas! Jesus Christ healeth thee! Arise, and smooth thy bed for thyself. And, straightway, he arose.
text.KJV: 9:34 And Peter said vnto him, Aeneas, Iesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.

text.NRSV: And all the residents of Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And all who dwelt in Lydda and Saron, saw him,—and, they, turned unto the Lord.
text.KJV: 9:35 And all that dwelt at Lydda, and Saron, saw him, and turned to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Now in Joppa there was a disciple whose name was Tabitha, which in Greek is Dorcas. She was devoted to good works and acts of charity.
text.Rotherham: Now, in Joppa, there was a certain female disciple, by name Tabitha, which, being translated, means Dorcas [a Gazelle] . The same, was full of good works and alms which she was doing.
text.KJV: 9:36 ¶ Now there was at Ioppa a certain disciple, named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas: This woman was full of good works, and almes deeds, which she did.

text.NRSV: At that time she became ill and died. When they had washed her, they laid her in a room upstairs.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in those days, that she, sickening, died; and, bathing her, they laid her in an upper room.
text.KJV: 9:37 And it came to passe in those dayes that she was sicke, and died: whome when they had washed, they laid her in an vpper chamber.

text.NRSV: Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, who heard that Peter was there, sent two men to him with the request, “Please come to us without delay.”
text.Rotherham: Now, Lydda being nigh unto Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was therein, sent off two men unto him, beseeching him—Do not delay to come through unto us!
text.KJV: 9:38 And forasmuch as Lydda was nigh to Ioppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent vnto him two men, desiring him that he would not delay to come to them.

text.NRSV: So Peter got up and went with them; and when he arrived, they took him to the room upstairs. All the widows stood beside him, weeping and showing tunics and other clothing that Dorcas had made while she was with them.
text.Rotherham: And Peter, arising, went with them,—whom, when he arrived, they brought up into the upper room; and there stood by him all the widows, weeping, and showing the tunics and mantles—whatsoever things Dorcas was making while she was with them.
text.KJV: 9:39 Then Peter arose and went with them: when he was come, they brought him into the vpper chamber: And all the widowes stood by him weeping, and shewing the coats and garments which Dorcas made, while shee was with them.

text.NRSV: Peter put all of them outside, and then he knelt down and prayed. He turned to the body and said, “Tabitha, get up.” Then she opened her eyes, and seeing Peter, she sat up.
text.Rotherham: But Peter, putting them all outside, knelt down and prayed; and, turning towards the body, said—Tabitha, arise! And she, opening her eyes and seeing Peter, sat up.
text.KJV: 9:40 But Peter put them all forth, and kneeled downe, and prayed, and turning him to the body, said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sate vp.

text.NRSV: He gave her his hand and helped her up. Then calling the saints and widows, he showed her to be alive.
text.Rotherham: And, giving her his hand, he raised her up; and, calling the saints and the widows, presented her, living.
text.KJV: 9:41 And he gaue her his hand, and lift her vp: and when hee had called the Saints & widowes, presented her aliue.

text.NRSV: This became known throughout Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And it became known throughout the whole of Joppa, and many believed upon the Lord.
text.KJV: 9:42 And it was knowen thorowout all Ioppa, and many beleeued in the Lord.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile he stayed in Joppa for some time with a certain Simon, a tanner.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, for a considerable number of days, he abode in Joppa, with one Simon, a tanner.
text.KJV: 9:43 And it came to passe, that he taried many dayes in Ioppa, with one Simon a Tanner.

text.NRSV: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of the Italian Cohort, as it was called.
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man in Caesarea, by name Cornelius, a centurion of the band called Italian,—
text.KJV: 10:1 There was a certaine man in Cesarea, called Cornelius, a Centurion of þ band called the Italian band,

text.NRSV: He was a devout man who feared God with all his household; he gave alms generously to the people and prayed constantly to God.
text.Rotherham: Devout, and fearing God with all his house, doing many alms unto the people, and supplicating God continually,
text.KJV: 10:2 A deuout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gaue much almes to the people, and prayed to God alway.

text.NRSV: One afternoon at about three o’clock he had a vision in which he clearly saw an angel of God coming in and saying to him, “Cornelius.”
text.Rotherham: Saw, in a vision, manifestly, as if about the ninth hour of the day, a messenger of God, coming in unto him, and saying unto him—Cornelius!
text.KJV: 10:3 He saw in a vision euidently, about the ninth houre of the day, an Angel of God comming in to him, and saying vnto him Cornelius.

text.NRSV: He stared at him in terror and said, “What is it, Lord?” He answered, “Your prayers and your alms have ascended as a memorial before God.
text.Rotherham: And he, looking steadfastly at him, and becoming full of fear, said—What is it, Lord? And he said unto him—Thy prayers and thine alms, have gone up for a memorial before God.
text.KJV: 10:4 And when he looked on him, hee was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said vnto him, Thy praiers and thine almes are come vp for a memorial before God.

text.NRSV: Now send men to Joppa for a certain Simon who is called Peter;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, send men unto Joppa, and fetch one Simon who is surnamed Peter,—
text.KJV: 10:5 And now send men to Ioppa, and call for one Simon, whose sirname is Peter.

text.NRSV: he is lodging with Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the seaside.”
text.Rotherham: The same is a guest with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea.
text.KJV: 10:6 Hee lodgeth with one Simon a Tanner, whose house is by the Sea side; he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to doe.

text.NRSV: When the angel who spoke to him had left, he called two of his slaves and a devout soldier from the ranks of those who served him,
text.Rotherham: And, when the messenger who had been speaking with him had departed, calling two of the domestics, and a devout soldier of them that constantly attended him,
text.KJV: 10:7 And when the Angel which spake vnto Cornelius, was departed, he called two of his houshold seruants, and a deuout souldier of them that waited on him continually.

text.NRSV: and after telling them everything, he sent them to Joppa.
text.Rotherham: And relating everything unto them, he sent them off unto Joppa.
text.KJV: 10:8 And when he had declared all these things vnto them, he sent them to Ioppa.

text.NRSV: About noon the next day, as they were on their journey and approaching the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray.
text.Rotherham: Now, on the morrow, as those men were journeying, and, unto the city, drawing near, Peter went up on the housetop to pray, about the sixth hour;
text.KJV: 10:9 ¶ On the morrow as they went on their iourney, and drew nigh vnto the citie, Peter went vp vpon the house to pray, about the sixth houre.

text.NRSV: He became hungry and wanted something to eat; and while it was being prepared, he fell into a trance.
text.Rotherham: But he became hungry, and wished to eat,—and, while they were making ready, there came upon him a trance;
text.KJV: 10:10 And he became very hungry, and would haue eaten: But while they made ready, he fell into a traunce,

text.NRSV: He saw the heaven opened and something like a large sheet coming down, being lowered to the ground by its four corners.
text.Rotherham: And he beholdeth heaven opened, and, corning down, a kind of vessel, like a large linen cloth, by its four corners, being let down upon the earth,
text.KJV: 10:11 And saw heauen opened, and a certaine vessell descending vnto him, as it had beene a great sheete, knit at the foure corners, and let downe to the earth:

text.NRSV: In it were all kinds of four-footed creatures and reptiles and birds of the air.
text.Rotherham: In which were all the quadrupeds and creeping things of earth and birds of heaven.
text.KJV: 10:12 Wherein were all maner of foure footed beasts of the earth, and wilde beasts, and creeping things, and foules of the ayre.

text.NRSV: Then he heard a voice saying, “Get up, Peter; kill and eat.”
text.Rotherham: And there came a voice unto him—Rise, Peter! slay and eat.
text.KJV: 10:13 And there came a voyce to him, Rise, Peter: kill, and eate.

text.NRSV: But Peter said, “By no means, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is profane or unclean.”
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—By no means, Lord! because, at no time, have I eaten anything common or unclean.
text.KJV: 10:14 But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I haue neuer eaten any thing that is common or vncleane.

text.NRSV: The voice said to him again, a second time, “What God has made clean, you must not call profane.”
text.Rotherham: And a voice [came] again, a second time, unto him—What things, God, hath cleansed, be not, thou, making common.
text.KJV: 10:15 And the voice spake vnto him againe the second time, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.

text.NRSV: This happened three times, and the thing was suddenly taken up to heaven.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, took place thrice; and, straightway, was the vessel taken up into heaven.
text.KJV: 10:16 This was done thrise: & the vessel was receiued vp againe into heauen.

text.NRSV: Now while Peter was greatly puzzled about what to make of the vision that he had seen, suddenly the men sent by Cornelius appeared. They were asking for Simon’s house and were standing by the gate.
text.Rotherham: And, as within himself Peter was doubting what the vision which he had seen might mean, lo! the men who had been sent by Cornelius, having sought out the house of Simon, stood at the gate,
text.KJV: 10:17 Now while Peter doubted in himselfe what this vision which he had seene, should meane: behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius, had made inquirie for Simons house, and stood before the gate,

text.NRSV: They called out to ask whether Simon, who was called Peter, was staying there.
text.Rotherham: And, calling, enquired whether, Simon who was surnamed Peter, was there being entertained.
text.KJV: 10:18 And called, and asked whether Simon, which was sirnamed Peter, were lodged there.

text.NRSV: While Peter was still pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Look, three men are searching for you.
text.Rotherham: And, as Peter was pondering over the vision, the Spirit said—Lo! two men, seeking thee.
text.KJV: 10:19 ¶ While Peter thought on the vision, the spirit said vnto him, Behold, three men seeke thee.

text.NRSV: Get up and go down, and go with them without hesitation; for I have sent them.”
text.Rotherham: But rise, go down, and be journeying with them, nothing, doubting; because, I, have sent them.
text.KJV: 10:20 Arise therefore, and get thee downe, and goe with them, doubting nothing: for I haue sent them.

text.NRSV: So Peter went down to the men and said, “I am the one you are looking for; what is the reason for your coming?”
text.Rotherham: And Peter, going down unto the men, said—Lo! I, am he whom ye are seeking: What is the cause, for which ye are come?
text.KJV: 10:21 Then Peter went downe to the men, which were sent vnto him from Cornelius, and said, Behold, I am hee, whom ye seeke: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?

text.NRSV: They answered, “Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man, who is well spoken of by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to send for you to come to his house and to hear what you have to say.”
text.Rotherham: And they said—Cornelius, a centurion, a man righteous and fearing God, well-attested by the whole nation of the Jews, hath been divinely instructed by a holy messenger to send for thee unto his house, and to hear words from thee.
text.KJV: 10:22 And they saide, Cornelius the Centurion, a iust man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Iewes, was warned from God by an holy Angel, to send for thee into his house, and to heare words of thee.

text.NRSV: So Peter invited them in and gave them lodging. The next day he got up and went with them, and some of the believers from Joppa accompanied him.
text.Rotherham: Inviting them in, therefore, he entertained them; but, on the morrow, he rose up and went forth with them, and certain of the brethren who were from Joppa went with him;
text.KJV: 10:23 Then called he them in, and lodged them: And on the morrowe Peter went away with them, and certaine brethren from Ioppa accōpanied him.

text.NRSV: The following day they came to Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
text.Rotherham: And, on the morrow, he entered into Caesarea. And, Cornelius, was expecting them, having called together his kinsfolk and intimate friends.
text.KJV: 10:24 And the morrow after they entred into Cesarea: and Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and neere friends.

text.NRSV: On Peter’s arrival Cornelius met him, and falling at his feet, worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And, when it came about that Peter entered, Cornelius met him, and, falling at his feet, did homage.
text.KJV: 10:25 And as Peter was comming in, Cornelius met him, and fell downe at his feete, and worshipped him.

text.NRSV: But Peter made him get up, saying, “Stand up; I am only a mortal.”
text.Rotherham: But, Peter, raised him up, saying—Arise! I also myself, am, a man.
text.KJV: 10:26 But Peter tooke him vp, saying, Stand vp, I my selfe also am a man.

text.NRSV: And as he talked with him, he went in and found that many had assembled;
text.Rotherham: And, conversing with him, he went in, and findeth many come together;
text.KJV: 10:27 And as he talked with him, hee went in, and found many that were come together.

text.NRSV: and he said to them, “You yourselves know that it is unlawful for a Jew to associate with or to visit a Gentile; but God has shown me that I should not call anyone profane or unclean.
text.Rotherham: And said unto the—Ye, well know, how unlawful it is, for, a Jew, to be joining himself or coming in unto one of another race. And yet, unto me, hath God pointed out that I should be calling no man, common or unclean.
text.KJV: 10:28 And he said vnto them, Ye know how that it is an vnlawfull thing for a man that is a Iewe, to keepe company or come vnto one of another nation: but God hath shewed me, that I should not call any man common or vncleane.

text.NRSV: So when I was sent for, I came without objection. Now may I ask why you sent for me?”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, even without gainsaying, came I when sent for. I ask, therefore, for what reason ye sent for me.
text.KJV: 10:29 Therfore came I vnto you without gainesaying, as soone as I was sent for. I aske therefore, for what intent ye haue sent for me.

text.NRSV: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago at this very hour, at three o’clock, I was praying in my house when suddenly a man in dazzling clothes stood before me.
text.Rotherham: And Cornelius said—Four days ago, counting unto this very hour, I was keeping, the ninth hour, as one of prayer, in my house. And lo! a man stood before me, in bright clothing,
text.KJV: 10:30 And Cornelius said, Foure daies agoe I was fasting vntill this houre, and at the ninth houre I prayed in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,

text.NRSV: He said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God.
text.Rotherham: And saith—Cornelius! thy prayer hath been heard, and, thine alms, have been remembered before God.
text.KJV: 10:31 And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine almes are had in remembrance in the sight of God.

text.NRSV: Send therefore to Joppa and ask for Simon, who is called Peter; he is staying in the home of Simon, a tanner, by the sea.’
text.Rotherham: Send, therefore, unto Joppa, and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter. The same is being entertained in the house of one Simon a tanner, by the sea.
text.KJV: 10:32 Send therfore to Ioppa, and call hither Simon, whose sirname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of one Simon a Tanner, by the Sea side, who when he cōmeth, shall speake vnto thee.

text.NRSV: Therefore I sent for you immediately, and you have been kind enough to come. So now all of us are here in the presence of God to listen to all that the Lord has commanded you to say.”
text.Rotherham: Immediately, therefore, I sent unto thee: Thou, also hast, well, done in coming. Now, therefore, all we, before God are present, to hear all things that have been enjoined upon thee by the Lord.
text.KJV: 10:33 Immediately therefore I sent to thee, and thou hast well done, that thou art come. Now therefore are we all heere present before God, to heare all things that are cōmanded thee of God.

text.NRSV: Then Peter began to speak to them: “I truly understand that God shows no partiality,
text.Rotherham: And Peter, opening his mouth, said—Of a truth, I find that God is no respecter of persons;
text.KJV: 10:34 ¶ Then Peter opened his mouth, and said, Of a trueth I perceiue þ God is no respecter of persons:

text.NRSV: but in every nation anyone who fears him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
text.Rotherham: But, in every nation, he that feareth him and worketh righteousness, is acceptable unto him.
text.KJV: 10:35 But in euery nation, he that feareth him, and worketh righteousnesse, is accepted with him.

text.NRSV: You know the message he sent to the people of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ—he is Lord of all.
text.Rotherham: As touching the word he hath sent unto the sons of Israel, announcing the glad tidings of peace through Jesus Christ—the same, is Lord, of all,
text.KJV: 10:36 The word which God sent vnto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Iesus Christ (he is Lord of all.)

text.NRSV: That message spread throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism that John announced:
text.Rotherham: Ye yourselves, know what hath come to pass throughout the whole of Judaea, beginning from Galilee, after the immersion which John proclaimed, respecting Jesus who was of Nazareth:—
text.KJV: 10:37 That word (I say) you knowe which was published thorowout all Iudea, and began from Galilee, after the baptisme which Iohn preached:

text.NRSV: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power; how he went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
text.Rotherham: How God anointed him with Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all that were oppressed by the adversary, because, God, was with him.
text.KJV: 10:38 How God anointed Iesus of Nazareth with the holy Ghost, and with power, who went about doing good, and healing all that were oppressed of the deuill: for God was with him.

text.NRSV: We are witnesses to all that he did both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a tree;
text.Rotherham: We also, are witnesses of all things which he did, both in the country of the Jews and Jerusalem; whom they even slew by suspending upon a tree;—
text.KJV: 10:39 And we are witnesses of all things which hee did both in the land of the Iewes, and in Hierusalem, whom they slew and hanged on a tree,

text.NRSV: but God raised him on the third day and allowed him to appear,
text.Rotherham: The same, God raised up on the third day, and gave him to become, manifest,
text.KJV: 10:40 Him God raised vp the third day, and shewed him openly,

text.NRSV: not to all the people but to us who were chosen by God as witnesses, and who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Not unto all the people, but unto witnesses who had been fore-appointed by God, unto us, who, indeed, did eat and drink with him after his rising from among the dead.
text.KJV: 10:41 Not to all the people, but vnto witnesses, chosen before of God, euen to vs who did eate and drinke with him after he rose from the dead.

text.NRSV: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one ordained by God as judge of the living and the dead.
text.Rotherham: And he charged us to proclaim unto the people, and bear full witness, that—This, is he that hath been marked out by God to be judge of living and dead.
text.KJV: 10:42 And he commanded vs to preach vnto the people, and to testifie that it is he which was ordeined of God to be the Iudge of quicke and dead.

text.NRSV: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
text.Rotherham: Unto the same, do all the prophets bear witness, That, remission of sins, is to be received through his name, by every one that believeth on him.
text.KJV: 10:43 To him giue all the Prophets witnesse, that through his Name whosoeuer beleeueth in him, shall receiue remission of sinnes.

text.NRSV: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who heard the word.
text.Rotherham: While Peter was yet speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who were hearing the word.
text.KJV: 10:44 ¶ While Peter yet spake these words, the holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.

text.NRSV: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astounded that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles,
text.Rotherham: And the faithful, of the circumcision, who had come with Peter, were amazed,—in that, upon the nations also, the free-gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out;
text.KJV: 10:45 And they of the circumcision which beleeued, were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Gentiles also was powred out the gift of the holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: for they heard them speaking in tongues and extolling God. Then Peter said,
text.Rotherham: For they heard them speaking with tongues, and magnifying God. Then answered Peter—
text.KJV: 10:46 For they heard them speake with tongues, and magnifie God. Then answered Peter,

text.NRSV: “Can anyone withhold the water for baptizing these people who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
text.Rotherham: Surely then, the water, can no man forbid, that these should not be immersed,—seeing that, the Holy Spirit, they have received, as well as we.
text.KJV: 10:47 Can any man forbid water, that these should not bee baptized, which haue receiued the holy Ghost, as well as wee?

text.NRSV: So he ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited him to stay for several days.
text.Rotherham: And he commanded them in the name of Jesus Christ to be immersed. Then, requested they him, to abide still some days.
text.KJV: 10:48 And hee commanded them to be baptized in the Name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarie certaine dayes.

text.NRSV: Now the apostles and the believers who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also accepted the word of God.
text.Rotherham: Now the Apostles and the brethren who were throughout Judaea heard that, the nations also, had welcomed the word of God.
text.KJV: 11:1 And the Apostles, and brethren that were in Iudea, heard that the Gentiles had also receiued the word of God.

text.NRSV: So when Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him,
text.Rotherham: And, when Peter came up unto Jerusalem, they of the circumcision began to find fault with him,
text.KJV: 11:2 And when Peter was come vp to Hierusalem, they that were of the circumcision contended with him,

text.NRSV: saying, “Why did you go to uncircumcised men and eat with them?”
text.Rotherham: Saying—he went in unto men uncircumcised, and did eat with them.
text.KJV: 11:3 Saying, Thou wentest in to men vncircumcised, & didst eate with them.

text.NRSV: Then Peter began to explain it to them, step by step, saying,
text.Rotherham: But Peter, making a beginning, went on to set forth the matter unto them in order, saying—
text.KJV: 11:4 But Peter rehearsed the matter from the beginning, and expounded it by order vnto them, saying,

text.NRSV: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision. There was something like a large sheet coming down from heaven, being lowered by its four corners; and it came close to me.
text.Rotherham: I was in the city of Joppa praying, and saw in a trance, a vision:—coming down a sort of vessel, like a large sheet, by four corners, being let down out of heaven, and it came even unto me:
text.KJV: 11:5 I was in the citie of Ioppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision, a certaine vessell descend, as it had beene a great sheete, let downe from heauen by foure corners, and it came euen to me.

text.NRSV: As I looked at it closely I saw four-footed animals, beasts of prey, reptiles, and birds of the air.
text.Rotherham: Into which steadfastly looking, began to observe, and saw the quadrupeds of the earth, and the wild beasts, and the creeping things, and the birds of heaven.
text.KJV: 11:6 Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw foure footed beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and foules of the aire.

text.NRSV: I also heard a voice saying to me, ‘Get up, Peter; kill and eat.’
text.Rotherham: Moreover heard a voice also, saying unto me—Rise, Peter! slay and eat.
text.KJV: 11:7 And I heard a voyce, saying vnto me, Arise Peter, slay, and eate.

text.NRSV: But I replied, ‘By no means, Lord; for nothing profane or unclean has ever entered my mouth.’
text.Rotherham: But I said—By no means, Lord, because, a common or unclean thing, hath at no time entered into my mouth.
text.KJV: 11:8 But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or vncleane hath at any time entred into my mouth.

text.NRSV: But a second time the voice answered from heaven, ‘What God has made clean, you must not call profane.’
text.Rotherham: And a voice answered, a second time, out of heaven—What things, God, hath cleansed, be not, thou, making common.
text.KJV: 11:9 But the voyce answered me againe from heauen, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.

text.NRSV: This happened three times; then everything was pulled up again to heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, this, took place thrice,—and the whole was drawn up again into heaven;
text.KJV: 11:10 And this was done three times: and all were drawen vp againe into heauen.

text.NRSV: At that very moment three men, sent to me from Caesarea, arrived at the house where we were.
text.Rotherham: And lo! immediately, three men halted at the house wherein we were, sent from Caesarea unto me.
text.KJV: 11:11 And behold, immediately there were three men already come vnto the house where I was, sent from Cesarea vnto me.

text.NRSV: The Spirit told me to go with them and not to make a distinction between them and us. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered the man’s house.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. And there went with me these six brethren also; and we entered into the house of the man,
text.KJV: 11:12 And the spirit bad me goe with them, nothing doubting: Moreouer, these sixe brethren accompanied me, and we entred into the mans house:

text.NRSV: He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house and saying, ‘Send to Joppa and bring Simon, who is called Peter;
text.Rotherham: And he related to us how he had seen the messenger in his house, standing, and saying—Send away unto Joppa, and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter;
text.KJV: 11:13 And he shewed vs how hee had seene an Angell in his house, which stood and said vnto him, Send men to Ioppa, and call for Simon, whose sirname is Peter:

text.NRSV: he will give you a message by which you and your entire household will be saved.’
text.Rotherham: Who shall speak words unto thee, whereby thou shall be saved, thou, and all thy house.
text.KJV: 11:14 Who shall tell thee words, wherby thou, and all thy house shal be saued.

text.NRSV: And as I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell upon them just as it had upon us at the beginning.
text.Rotherham: And, as I began to be speaking, the Holy Spirit fell upon them, just as upon us also at the beginning.
text.KJV: 11:15 And as I began to speake, the holy Ghost fell on them, as on vs at the beginning.

text.NRSV: And I remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said, ‘John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’
text.Rotherham: And I was put in mind of the word of the Lord, how he used to say—John, indeed, immersed with water; but, ye, shall be immersed in Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 11:16 Then remembred I the word of the Lord, how that he said, Iohn indeede baptized with water: but ye shall be baptized with the holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: If then God gave them the same gift that he gave us when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I that I could hinder God?”
text.Rotherham: If therefore the like free-gift God gave unto them, as even unto us, when we had believed upon the Lord Jesus Christ, who was, I, that could withstand God?
text.KJV: 11:17 Forasmuch then as God gaue them the like gift as hee did vnto vs, who beleeued on the Lord Iesus Christ: what was I that I could withstand God?

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were silenced. And they praised God, saying, “Then God has given even to the Gentiles the repentance that leads to life.”
text.Rotherham: And, having heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying—Hence, even unto the nations, God hath granted repentance unto life.
text.KJV: 11:18 When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance vnto life.

text.NRSV: Now those who were scattered because of the persecution that took place over Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, and they spoke the word to no one except Jews.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, who had been scattered abroad by reason of the tribulation that took place on account of Stephen, passed through as far as Phoenice and Cyprus and Antioch, unto no one speaking the word, save alone unto Jews.
text.KJV: 11:19 ¶ Now they which were scattered abroad vpon the persecution that arose about Steuen, trauailed as farre as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none, but vnto the Iewes onely.

text.NRSV: But among them were some men of Cyprus and Cyrene who, on coming to Antioch, spoke to the Hellenists also, proclaiming the Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And there were some from among them, Cyprians and Cyrenians, who, indeed, coming unto Antioch, began speaking, even unto the Grecian Jews, announcing the glad tidings as to the Lord Jesus;
text.KJV: 11:20 And some of them were men of Cyprus, and Cyrene, which when they were come to Antioch, spake vnto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Iesus.

text.NRSV: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number became believers and turned to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number—they who believed—turned unto the Lord.
text.KJV: 11:21 And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number beleeued, and turned vnto the Lord.

text.NRSV: News of this came to the ears of the church in Jerusalem, and they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
text.Rotherham: And the matter was reported in the hearing of the assembly that was in Jerusalem, concerning them; and they sent forth Barnabas, as far as Antioch;
text.KJV: 11:22 ¶ Then tidings of these things came vnto the eares of the Church, which was in Hierusalem: and they sent foorth Barnabas, that hee should goe as farre as Antioch.

text.NRSV: When he came and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced, and he exhorted them all to remain faithful to the Lord with steadfast devotion;
text.Rotherham: Who, arriving, and seeing the favour which was of God, rejoiced, and went on to beseech all, with the purpose of their heart, to abide [in] the Lord;
text.KJV: 11:23 Who when hee came, and had seene the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleaue vnto the Lord.

text.NRSV: for he was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and of faith. And a great many people were brought to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Because he was a good man, and full of Holy Spirit and faith; and a considerable multitude were added unto the Lord.
text.KJV: 11:24 For he was a good man, and full of the holy Ghost, and of faith: and much people was added vnto the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul,
text.Rotherham: He went away, however, unto Tarsus, to seek up Saul;
text.KJV: 11:25 Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seeke Saul.

text.NRSV: and when he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. So it was that for an entire year they met with the church and taught a great many people, and it was in Antioch that the disciples were first called “Christians.”
text.Rotherham: And, finding him, he brought him unto Antioch. And so it was with them, that, for a whole year, they were brought together in the assembly, and taught a considerable multitude; also that the disciples, first in Antioch, were called, Christians.
text.KJV: 11:26 And when he had found him, he brought him vnto Antioch. And it came to passe, that a whole yeere they assembled themselues with the Church, and taught much people, and the disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.

text.NRSV: At that time prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
text.Rotherham: Now, in these days, there came down from Jerusalem, prophets, unto Antioch.
text.KJV: 11:27 ¶ And in these dayes, came Prophets from Hierusalem vnto Antioch.

text.NRSV: One of them named Agabus stood up and predicted by the Spirit that there would be a severe famine over all the world; and this took place during the reign of Claudius.
text.Rotherham: And one from among them, by name Agabus, rising up, gave a sign, through means of the Spirit, that, a great famine, was coming over all the inhabited earth; which, indeed, came to pass under Claudius.
text.KJV: 11:28 And there stood vp one of them, named Agabus, and signified by the spirit, that there should be great dearth throughout all the world: which came to passe in the dayes of Claudius Cesar.

text.NRSV: The disciples determined that according to their ability, each would send relief to the believers living in Judea;
text.Rotherham: And, according as any one of the disciples was being prospered, they each one of them set apart [something] for ministering, to send unto the brethren, who dwelt in Jerusalem;—
text.KJV: 11:29 Then the disciples, euery man according to his abilitie, determined to send reliefe vnto the brethren which dwelt in Iudea.

text.NRSV: this they did, sending it to the elders by Barnabas and Saul.
text.Rotherham: Which thing they also did, sending it unto the Elders, through the hand of Barnabas and Saul.
text.KJV: 11:30 Which also they did, and sent it to the Elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

text.NRSV: About that time King Herod laid violent hands upon some who belonged to the church.
text.Rotherham: Now, in the course of that season, Herod the king thrust forth his hands to harm some of them of the assembly,—
text.KJV: 12:1 Now about that time, Herode the King stretched foorth his hands, to vexe certaine of the Church.

text.NRSV: He had James, the brother of John, killed with the sword.
text.Rotherham: And slew James the brother of John with a sword;
text.KJV: 12:2 And he killed Iames the brother of Iohn with the sword.

text.NRSV: After he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. (This was during the festival of Unleavened Bread.)
text.Rotherham: And, seeing that it was acceptable unto the Jews, he went on to apprehend Peter also (now they were the days of unleavened bread),—
text.KJV: 12:3 And because he saw it pleased the Iewes, hee proceeded further, to take Peter also. (Then were the dayes of vnleauened bread.)

text.NRSV: When he had seized him, he put him in prison and handed him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
text.Rotherham: Whom also having seized, he put into prison, delivering him up unto four quaternions of soldiers, to be guarding him,—intending, after the passover, to bring him up unto the people.
text.KJV: 12:4 And when hee had apprehended him, hee put him in prison, and deliuered him to foure quaternions of souldiers to keepe him, intending after Easter to bring him forth to the people.

text.NRSV: While Peter was kept in prison, the church prayed fervently to God for him.
text.Rotherham: Peter, therefore, was kept in the prison; but, prayer, was, earnestly, being made by the assembly, unto God, concerning him.
text.KJV: 12:5 Peter therefore was kept in prison, but prayer was made without ceasing of the Church vnto God for him.

text.NRSV: The very night before Herod was going to bring him out, Peter, bound with two chains, was sleeping between two soldiers, while guards in front of the door were keeping watch over the prison.
text.Rotherham: And, when Herod was about to bring him forth, on that night, was Peter sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, guards, also, before the door, were keeping the prison.
text.KJV: 12:6 And when Herode would haue brought him foorth, the same night Peter was sleeping betweene two Souldiers, bound with two chaines, and the Keepers before the doore kept the prison.

text.NRSV: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his wrists.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a messenger of the Lord stood over him, and a light shone in the cell; and, smiting the side of Peter, he roused him up, saying—Rise up quickly! And his chains fell off out of his hands.
text.KJV: 12:7 And beholde, the Angel of the Lord came vpon him, and a light shined in the prison: and hee smote Peter on the side, and raised him vp, saying, Arise vp quickely. And his chaines fell off from his hands.

text.NRSV: The angel said to him, “Fasten your belt and put on your sandals.” He did so. Then he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto him—Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And he did so. And he saith unto him—Throw around thee thy mantle, and be following me.
text.KJV: 12:8 And the Angel said vnto him, Girde thy selfe, and binde on thy sandales: And so he did. And he sayth vnto him, Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.

text.NRSV: Peter went out and followed him; he did not realize that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
text.Rotherham: And, coming out, he began following, and knew not that it was true which was coming about through means of the messenger; but supposed that, a vision, he was beholding.
text.KJV: 12:9 And hee went out, and followed him, and wist not that it was true which was done by the Angel: but thought he saw a vision.

text.NRSV: After they had passed the first and the second guard, they came before the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went outside and walked along a lane, when suddenly the angel left him.
text.Rotherham: And, passing through the first ward and the second, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth into the city, the which, of its own accord, opened unto them; and, coming out, they went on through one street, and, straightway, the messenger was parted from him.
text.KJV: 12:10 When they were past the first and the second ward, they came vnto the yron gate that leadeth vnto the citie, which opened to them of his owne accord: and they went out and passed on thorow one streete, and foorthwith the Angel departed from him.

text.NRSV: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hands of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, coming to himself said—Now, know I, of a truth, that the Lord hath sent forth his messenger, and taken me out of the hand of Herod, and all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
text.KJV: 12:11 And when Peter was come to himselfe, hee said, Now I know of a suretie, that the Lord hath sent his Angel, and hath deliuered mee out of the hand of Herode, and from all the expectation of the people of the Iewes.

text.NRSV: As soon as he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many had gathered and were praying.
text.Rotherham: And, considering the matter, he came unto the house of Mary, the mother of John who was surnamed Mark, where a considerable number were gathered together and praying.
text.KJV: 12:12 And when he had considered the thing, he came to the house of Mary the mother of Iohn whose sirname was Marke, where many were gathered together praying.

text.NRSV: When he knocked at the outer gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had knocked at the door of the porch, there came unto it a maiden to hearken, by name Rhoda;
text.KJV: 12:13 And as Peter knocked at the doore of the gate, a damosell came to hearken, named Rhoda.

text.NRSV: On recognizing Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that, instead of opening the gate, she ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
text.Rotherham: And, recognizing the voice of Peter, by reason of her joy, she opened not the porch,—but, running in, bare tidings that Peter was standing before the porch. But, they, unto her, said—Thou art raving
text.KJV: 12:14 And when she knew Peters voice, she opened not the gate for gladnes, but ran in, and told how Peter stood before the gate.

text.NRSV: They said to her, “You are out of your mind!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”
text.Rotherham: She, however, kept on strongly declaring that, so, it was. But, they, were saying—It is his, messenger.
text.KJV: 12:15 And they said vnto her, Thou art mad. But she constantly affirmed that it was euen so. Then said they, It it his Angel.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the gate, they saw him and were amazed.
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, continued knocking; and, opening, they saw him, and were amazed.
text.KJV: 12:16 But Peter continued knocking: and when they had opened the doore, and saw him, they were astonished.

text.NRSV: He motioned to them with his hand to be silent, and described for them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he added, “Tell this to James and to the believers.” Then he left and went to another place.
text.Rotherham: But, making a sign to them with his hand to hold their peace, he related to them how, the Lord, had brought, him, forth out of the prison; and he said—Carry tidings unto James and the brethren, as to these things. And, going out, he went his way unto some other place.
text.KJV: 12:17 But he beckening vnto them with the hand, to hold their peace, declared vnto them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison: And he said, Goe shew these things vnto Iames, and to the brethren. And he departed, and went into another place.

text.NRSV: When morning came, there was no small commotion among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
text.Rotherham: And, when it became day, there was no small commotion among the soldiers, as to what, then, Peter had become!
text.KJV: 12:18 Now assoone as it was day, there was no smal stirre among the souldiers, what was become of Peter.

text.NRSV: When Herod had searched for him and could not find him, he examined the guards and ordered them to be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
text.Rotherham: And, Herod, seeking after and not finding, him, having examined the guards, ordered them to be led away [to death] ; and, going down from Judaea unto Caesarea, stayed there.
text.KJV: 12:19 And when Herode had sought for him, and found him not, hee examined the keepers, and commanded that they should be put to death. And hee went downe from Iudea to Cesarea, & there abode.

text.NRSV: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they came to him in a body; and after winning over Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they asked for a reconciliation, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.
text.Rotherham: Now he was bitterly hostile to them of Tyre and Zidon; but, with one accord, they came unto him, and, persuading Blastus, who was over the bed-chamber of the king, they were suing for peace; because their country was fed by the king’s,
text.KJV: 12:20 ¶ And Herode was highly displeased with them of Tyre and Sidon: but they came with one accord to him, and hauing made Blastus the kings chamberlaine their friend, desired peace, because their countrey was nourished by the kings countrey.

text.NRSV: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat on the platform, and delivered a public address to them.
text.Rotherham: And, on an appointed day, Herod, putting on royal apparel, and seating himself upon the tribunal, proceeded to deliver an oration unto them.
text.KJV: 12:21 And vpon a set day Herod arayed in royall apparell, sate vpon his throne, and made an Oration vnto them.

text.NRSV: The people kept shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a mortal!”
text.Rotherham: And, the populace, began to shout—A god’s, voice, and not, a man’s!
text.KJV: 12:22 And the people gaue a shout, saying, It is the voice of a God, and not of a man.

text.NRSV: And immediately, because he had not given the glory to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
text.Rotherham: And, instantly, there smote him, a messenger of the Lord, because he gave not the glory unto God; and, becoming worm-eaten, he expired.
text.KJV: 12:23 And immediatly the Angel of the Lord smote him, because hee gaue not God the glory, and hee was eaten of wormes, and gaue vp the ghost.

text.NRSV: But the word of God continued to advance and gain adherents.
text.Rotherham: And, the word of the Lord, went on growing and multiplying.
text.KJV: 12:24 ¶ But the word of God grewe, and multiplied.

text.NRSV: Then after completing their mission, Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem and brought with them John, whose other name was Mark.
text.Rotherham: And, Barnabas and Saul, returned unto Jerusalem, fulfilling the ministering, taking with them John who was surnamed Mark.
text.KJV: 12:25 And Barnabas and Saul returned from Hierusalem, when they had fulfilled their ministerie, and tooke with them Iohn, whose syrname was Marke.

text.NRSV: Now in the church at Antioch there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen a member of the court of Herod the ruler, and Saul.
text.Rotherham: Now there were in Antioch, distributed through the existing assembly,—prophets and teachers: both Barnabas and Symeon who was called Niger, and Lucius the Cyrenian, Manaen also, Herod the tetrarch’s foster-brother, and Saul.
text.KJV: 13:1 Nowe there were in the Church that was at Antioch, certaine Prophets and teachers: as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had bene brought vp with Herod the Tetrarch, and Saul.

text.NRSV: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were publicly ministering unto the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said—Separate forthwith unto me, Barnabas and Saul, unto the work whereunto I have called them.
text.KJV: 13:2 As they ministred to the Lord, and fasted, the holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul, for the worke whereunto I haue called them.

text.NRSV: Then after fasting and praying they laid their hands on them and sent them off.
text.Rotherham: Then, fasting and praying, and laying their hands upon them, they sent them away.
text.KJV: 13:3 And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid their handes on them, they sent them away.

text.NRSV: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia; and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, being sent forth by the Holy Spirit, went down unto Seleucia, and, from thence, sailed away unto Cyprus;
text.KJV: 13:4 ¶ So they being sent forth by the holy Ghost, departed vnto Seleucia, and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.

text.NRSV: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews. And they had John also to assist them.
text.Rotherham: And, coming to be in Salamis, they declared the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews;—and they had, John also, as an attendant.
text.KJV: 13:5 And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the Synagogues of the Iewes: and they had also Iohn to their Minister.

text.NRSV: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they met a certain magician, a Jewish false prophet named Bar-Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, passing through the whole island, as far as Paphos, they found a certain man, a magician, a false-prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus;
text.KJV: 13:6 And when they had gone thorow the Ile vnto Paphos, they found a certaine sorcerer, a false prophet, a Iewe, whose name was Bariesus:

text.NRSV: He was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man, who summoned Barnabas and Saul and wanted to hear the word of God.
text.Rotherham: Who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man. The same, calling for Barnabas and Saul, sought to hear the word of God.
text.KJV: 13:7 Which was with the deputie of the countrey Sergius Paulus, a prudent man: who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to heare the word of God.

text.NRSV: But the magician Elymas (for that is the translation of his name) opposed them and tried to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
text.Rotherham: But Elymas the magician,—for so, when translated, is his name,—withstood them; seeking to turn aside the proconsul from the faith.
text.KJV: 13:8 But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turne away the deputy from the faith.

text.NRSV: But Saul, also known as Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked intently at him
text.Rotherham: But Saul, who is also Paul, filled with Holy Spirit, looking steadfastly at him,
text.KJV: 13:9 Then Saul (who also is called Paul) filled with the holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,

text.NRSV: and said, “You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy, will you not stop making crooked the straight paths of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Said—O full of all guile, and all recklessness! Son of an adversary! Enemy of all righteousness!—Wilt thou not cease to pervert the straight ways of the Lord?
text.KJV: 13:10 And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischiefe, thou child of the deuil, thou enemie of all righteousnesse, wilt thou not cease to peruert the right wayes of the Lord?

text.NRSV: And now listen—the hand of the Lord is against you, and you will be blind for a while, unable to see the sun.” Immediately mist and darkness came over him, and he went about groping for someone to lead him by the hand.
