text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun, until a fitting time. And, instantly, there fell upon him a mist and darkness; and, going about, he was seeking such as might lead him by the hand.
text.KJV: 13:11 And now behold, the hand of the Lord is vpon thee, & thou shalt be blind, not seeing the Sunne for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkenes, and he went about, seeking some to lead him by the hand.

text.NRSV: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed, for he was astonished at the teaching about the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then, the proconsul, seeing what had happened, believed, being amazed at the teaching of the Lord.
text.KJV: 13:12 Then the Deputie when he sawe what was done, beleeued, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Paul and his companions set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John, however, left them and returned to Jerusalem;
text.Rotherham: And, setting sail from Paphos, Paul’s company came into Perga of Pamphylia; but, John, withdrawing from them, returned unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 13:13 Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and Iohn departing from them, returned to Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: but they went on from Perga and came to Antioch in Pisidia. And on the sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
text.Rotherham: They, however, passing through from Perga, arrived at Antioch of Pisidia; and, going into the synagogue on the sabbath-day, sat down.
text.KJV: 13:14 ¶ But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and sate downe.

text.NRSV: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the officials of the synagogue sent them a message, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, give it.”
text.Rotherham: And, after the reading of the law and the prophets, the synagogue-rulers sent unto them, saying—Brethren! if there is in you a word of exhortation unto the people, say on.
text.KJV: 13:15 And after the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent vnto them, saying, Ye men and brethren, if ye haue any word of exhortation for the people, say on.

text.NRSV: So Paul stood up and with a gesture of his hand said, “You Israelites, and others who fear God, listen.
text.Rotherham: And Paul, standing up, and making a sign with his hand, said—Ye men of Israel! and such as revere God! hearken:—
text.KJV: 13:16 Then Paul stood vp, and beckning with his hand, said, Men of Israel, and ye that feare God, giue audience.

text.NRSV: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors and made the people great during their stay in the land of Egypt, and with uplifted arm he led them out of it.
text.Rotherham: The God of this people Israel, chose our fathers, and, the people, he exalted, by their sojourn in the land of Egypt,—and, with a high arm, brought he them out of it;
text.KJV: 13:17 The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arme brought he them out of it.

text.NRSV: For about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And, for the time of about forty years, bare with their manners in the desert;
text.KJV: 13:18 And about the time of fourtie yeeres suffered he their maners in the wildernesse.

text.NRSV: After he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance
text.Rotherham: And, overthrowing seven nations in the land of Canaan, gave them their land as an inheritance—about four hundred and fifty years.
text.KJV: 13:19 And when he had destroyed seuen nations in the land of Chanaan, he diuided their land to them by lot:

text.NRSV: for about four hundred fifty years. After that he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, he gave them judges, until Samuel the prophet.
text.KJV: 13:20 And after that he gaue vnto them iudges, about the space of foure hundred and fifty yeeres vntill Samuel the Prophet.

text.NRSV: Then they asked for a king; and God gave them Saul son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, who reigned for forty years.
text.Rotherham: And, from that time, they asked for themselves a king, and God gave them Saul, son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, during forty years;
text.KJV: 13:21 And afterward they desired a King, and God gaue vnto them Saul the sonne of Cis, a man of the tribe of Beniamin, by the space of fourty yeres.

text.NRSV: When he had removed him, he made David their king. In his testimony about him he said, ‘I have found David, son of Jesse, to be a man after my heart, who will carry out all my wishes.’
text.Rotherham: And, setting him aside, raised up, David, unto them for king,—of whom he also said, bearing witness—I have found David, the son of Jesse,—[a man] according to my heart, who will do all my will.
text.KJV: 13:22 And when he had remoued him, hee raised vp vnto them Dauid to be their king, to whom also he gaue testimonie, and said, I haue found Dauid the sonne of Iesse, a man after mine own heart, which shal fulfill all my wil.

text.NRSV: Of this man’s posterity God has brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus, as he promised;
text.Rotherham: From this man’s seed, hath God, according to promise, brought unto Israel, a saviour—Jesus:
text.KJV: 13:23 Of this mans seed hath God, according to his promise, raised vnto Israel a Sauiour, Iesus:

text.NRSV: before his coming John had already proclaimed a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: John, beforehand proclaiming, before the face of his coming in, an immersion of repentance, unto all the people of Israel.
text.KJV: 13:24 When Iohn had first preached before his coming, the baptisme of repentance to all the people of Israel.

text.NRSV: And as John was finishing his work, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. No, but one is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of the sandals on his feet.’
text.Rotherham: And, as John was fulfilling his course, he was saying—Whom are ye supposing that I am? I, am not he! But lo! there cometh, after me, one of whom I am not worthy, the sandals of his feet, to loosen.
text.KJV: 13:25 And as Iohn fulfilled his course, he said, Whom thinke ye that I am? I am not he. But behold, there commeth one after me, whose shooes of his feete I am not worthy to loose.

text.NRSV: “My brothers, you descendants of Abraham’s family, and others who fear God, to us the message of this salvation has been sent.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! sons of the race of Abraham, and those who among you revere God,—unto you, hath this word of salvation been sent forth.
text.KJV: 13:26 Men and brethren, children of the stocke of Abraham, and whosoeuer among you feareth God, to you is the word of this saluation sent.

text.NRSV: Because the residents of Jerusalem and their leaders did not recognize him or understand the words of the prophets that are read every sabbath, they fulfilled those words by condemning him.
text.Rotherham: For, they who were dwelling in Jerusalem, and their rulers, not recognising him, have, by judging him, fulfilled, the very voices of the prophets which every sabbath are being read;
text.KJV: 13:27 For they that dwell at Hierusalem, & their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the Prophets which are read euery Sabbath day, they haue fulfilled them in condemning him.

text.NRSV: Even though they found no cause for a sentence of death, they asked Pilate to have him killed.
text.Rotherham: And, though no single cause of death they found, yet claimed they of Pilate that he should be slain.
text.KJV: 13:28 And though they found no cause of death in him, yet desired they Pilate that he should be slaine.

text.NRSV: When they had carried out everything that was written about him, they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had finished all those things which, concerning him, had been written, taking him down from the tree, they put him in a tomb.
text.KJV: 13:29 And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they tooke him downe from the tree, and layd him in a Sepulchre.

text.NRSV: But God raised him from the dead;
text.Rotherham: But, God, raised him from among the dead:
text.KJV: 13:30 But God raised him frō the dead:

text.NRSV: and for many days he appeared to those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, and they are now his witnesses to the people.
text.Rotherham: Who appeared, during many days, unto them who had come up with him from Galilee unto Jerusalem; who, indeed, [now] are his witnesses unto the people.
text.KJV: 13:31 And he was seene many dayes of them which came vp with him from Galilee to Hierusalem, who are his witnesses vnto the people.

text.NRSV: And we bring you the good news that what God promised to our ancestors
text.Rotherham: We, therefore, unto you, bring the good news, as to the promise which, unto our fathers, was made,—
text.KJV: 13:32 And we declare vnto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made vnto the fathers,

text.NRSV: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising Jesus; as also it is written in the second psalm, ‘You are my Son; today I have begotten you.’
text.Rotherham: That God hath fulfilled, the same, for our children, by raising up Jesus: as also, in the second psalm, it is written—My son, art, thou: I, this day, have begotten thee.
text.KJV: 13:33 God hath fulfilled the same vnto vs their children, in that he hath raised vp Iesus againe, as it is also written in the second Psalme: Thou art my Sonne, this day haue I begotten thee.

text.NRSV: As to his raising him from the dead, no more to return to corruption, he has spoken in this way, ‘I will give you the holy promises made to David.’
text.Rotherham: And, in that he raised him from among the dead, no more destined to return unto corruption, on this wise hath he spoken—I will give unto you the faithful lovingkindnesses of David.
text.KJV: 13:34 And as concerning that he raised him vp from the dead, now no more to returne to corruption, he said on this wise, I will giue you the sure mercies of Dauid.

text.NRSV: Therefore he has also said in another psalm, ‘You will not let your Holy One experience corruption.’
text.Rotherham: Wherefore also, in a different [place], he saith—Thou wilt not give thy man of lovingkindness to see corruption.
text.KJV: 13:35 Wherfore he saith also in another Psalme, Thou shalt not suffer thine holy one to see corruption.

text.NRSV: For David, after he had served the purpose of God in his own generation, died, was laid alongside his ancestors, and experienced corruption;
text.Rotherham: For, David, indeed, unto his own generation having done service, by the counsel of God, fell asleep, and was added unto fathers, and saw corruption;
text.KJV: 13:36 For Dauid after he had serued his owne generation by the will of God, fell on sleepe, and was laide vnto his fathers, and saw corruption:

text.NRSV: but he whom God raised up experienced no corruption.
text.Rotherham: But, he whom God hath raised, did not see corruption.
text.KJV: 13:37 But hee whom God raised againe, saw no corruption.

text.NRSV: Let it be known to you therefore, my brothers, that through this man forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you;
text.Rotherham: Be it known unto you, therefore, brethren,—that, through this man, unto you, remission of sins is declared;
text.KJV: 13:38 ¶ Be it knowen vnto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached vnto you the forgiuenesse of sinnes.

text.NRSV: by this Jesus everyone who believes is set free from all those sins from which you could not be freed by the law of Moses.
text.Rotherham: And, from all things from which ye could not, by the law of Moses, be justified, by this man, everyone that believeth, is justified.
text.KJV: 13:39 And by him all þ beleeue, are iustified from all things, from which ye could not be iustified by the Law of Moses.

text.NRSV: Beware, therefore, that what the prophets said does not happen to you:
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed, therefore, lest that come upon you which hath been spoken in the prophets—
text.KJV: 13:40 Beware therefore, least that come vpon you which is spoken of in the Prophets,

text.NRSV: ‘Look, you scoffers! Be amazed and perish, for in your days I am doing a work, a work that you will never believe, even if someone tells you.’ ”
text.Rotherham: See, ye despisers, and marvel, and disappear: in that, a work, am, I, working in your days,—a work, which in nowise will ye believe, though one relate it in full unto you.
text.KJV: 13:41 Behold, yee despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I worke a worke in your dayes, a worke which you shall in no wise beleeue, though a man declare it vnto you.

text.NRSV: As Paul and Barnabas were going out, the people urged them to speak about these things again the next sabbath.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were going out, they kept on beseeching that, on the ensuing sabbath, might be spoken unto them these things.
text.KJV: 13:42 And when the Iewes were gone out of the Synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next Sabbath.

text.NRSV: When the meeting of the synagogue broke up, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them and urged them to continue in the grace of God.
text.Rotherham: And, when the congregation was broken up, there followed many of the Jews, and of the devout proselytes, with Paul and Barnabas; who, indeed, in speaking unto them, went on persuading them to abide in the favour of God.
text.KJV: 13:43 Now when the Congregation was broken vp, many of the Iewes, and religious Proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas, who speaking to them, perswaded them to continue in the grace of God.

text.NRSV: The next sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, on the coming sabbath, almost all the city, was gathered together, to hear the word of God.
text.KJV: 13:44 ¶ And the next Sabbath day came almost the whole citie together to heare the word of God.

text.NRSV: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy; and blaspheming, they contradicted what was spoken by Paul.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jews, seeing, the multitudes, were filled with jealousy,—and began speaking against the things which, by Paul, were being spoken, defaming them.
text.KJV: 13:45 But when the Iewes saw the multitudes, they were filled with enuie, and spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting, and blaspheming.

text.NRSV: Then both Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, saying, “It was necessary that the word of God should be spoken first to you. Since you reject it and judge yourselves to be unworthy of eternal life, we are now turning to the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: And Paul and Barnabas, speaking boldly, said—Unto you, was it necessary, that the word of God should first be spoken: seeing ye are thrusting it from you, and, unworthy, are judging yourselves of the age-abiding life, lo! we turn unto the nations;
text.KJV: 13:46 Then Paul and Barnabas waxed bold, and said, It was necessary that the word of God should first haue bene spoken to you: but seeing yee put it from you, and iudge your selues vnworthy of euerlasting life, loe, we turne to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: For so the Lord has commanded us, saying, ‘I have set you to be a light for the Gentiles, so that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’ ”
text.Rotherham: For so hath the Lord commanded us—I have set thee for a light of nations, that thou mayest be for salvation unto the end of the earth.
text.KJV: 13:47 For so hath the Lord cōmanded vs, saying, I haue set thee to bee a light of the Gentiles, that thou shouldest be for saluation vnto the ends of the earth.

text.NRSV: When the Gentiles heard this, they were glad and praised the word of the Lord; and as many as had been destined for eternal life became believers.
text.Rotherham: And they of the nations, hearing [this], began to rejoice, and to be glorifying God, and they believed—as many as had become disposed for life age-abiding.
text.KJV: 13:48 And when the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of the Lord: and as many as were ordeined to eternall life, beleeued.

text.NRSV: Thus the word of the Lord spread throughout the region.
text.Rotherham: And the word of the Lord went on to be carried through the whole country.
text.KJV: 13:49 And the word of the Lord was published throughout all the region.

text.NRSV: But the Jews incited the devout women of high standing and the leading men of the city, and stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out of their region.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jews, urged on the devout women of the higher class, and the chief men of the city, and roused up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas,—and thrust them out from their bounds.
text.KJV: 13:50 But the Iewes stirred vp the deuout and honourable women, and the chiefe men of the citie, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them out of their coasts.

text.NRSV: So they shook the dust off their feet in protest against them, and went to Iconium.
text.Rotherham: But they, shaking off the dust of their feet against them, came into Iconium.
text.KJV: 13:51 But they shooke off the dust of their feete against them, and came vnto Iconium.

text.NRSV: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, were filled with joy, and with Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 13:52 And the disciples were filled with ioy, and with the holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: The same thing occurred in Iconium, where Paul and Barnabas went into the Jewish synagogue and spoke in such a way that a great number of both Jews and Greeks became believers.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in Iconium that they together entered into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spake that there believed, both of Jews and Greeks, a great throng.
text.KJV: 14:1 And it came to passe in Iconium, that they went both together into the synagogue of the Iewes, and so spake, that a great multitude both of the Iewes, and also of the Greekes, beleeued.

text.NRSV: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles and poisoned their minds against the brothers.
text.Rotherham: But, the unpersuaded Jews, roused up and provoked the souls of them of the nations against the brethren.
text.KJV: 14:2 But the vnbeleeuing Iewes stirred vp the Gentiles, and made their mindes euill affected against the brethren.

text.NRSV: So they remained for a long time, speaking boldly for the Lord, who testified to the word of his grace by granting signs and wonders to be done through them.
text.Rotherham: A good while, therefore, tarried they, using boldness of speech [in dependence] upon the Lord, who was bearing witness unto his word of favour, granting signs and wonders to be coming to pass through their hands.
text.KJV: 14:3 Long time therefore abode they speaking boldly in the Lord, which gaue testimonie vnto the word of his grace, and granted signes and wonders to be done by their hands.

text.NRSV: But the residents of the city were divided; some sided with the Jews, and some with the apostles.
text.Rotherham: And the throng of the city was divided; and some indeed were with the Jews, while some were with the apostles.
text.KJV: 14:4 But the multitude of the city was diuided: and part held with the Iewes, and part with the Apostles.

text.NRSV: And when an attempt was made by both Gentiles and Jews, with their rulers, to mistreat them and to stone them,
text.Rotherham: But, when there took place an assault both of them of the nations and of the Jews, with their rulers, to maltreat and to stone them,
text.KJV: 14:5 And when there was an assault made both of the Gentiles, and also of the Iewes, with their rulers, to vse them despitefully, and to stone them,

text.NRSV: the apostles learned of it and fled to Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and to the surrounding country;
text.Rotherham: They became aware of it, and fled unto the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra and Derbe, and the surrounding country;
text.KJV: 14:6 They were ware of it, and fled vnto Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and vnto the region that lyeth round about.

text.NRSV: and there they continued proclaiming the good news.
text.Rotherham: And, there, were they announcing the glad tidings.
text.KJV: 14:7 And there they preached the Gospell.

text.NRSV: In Lystra there was a man sitting who could not use his feet and had never walked, for he had been lame from birth.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain man in Lystra, impotent in his feet, was sitting,—lame from his mother’s womb, who never had walked.
text.KJV: 14:8 ¶ And there sate a certaine man at Lystra, impotent in his feete, being a creeple from his mothers wombe, who neuer had walked.

text.NRSV: He listened to Paul as he was speaking. And Paul, looking at him intently and seeing that he had faith to be healed,
text.Rotherham: This man, hearkened unto Paul, as he was speaking,—who, looking steadfastly at him, and seeing he had faith to be made well,
text.KJV: 14:9 The same heard Paul speake: who stedfastly beholding him, and perceiuing that he had faith to be healed,

text.NRSV: said in a loud voice, “Stand upright on your feet.” And the man sprang up and began to walk.
text.Rotherham: Said, with a loud voice—Stand up on thy feet, erect! And he sprang up, and began to walk about.
text.KJV: 14:10 Said with a lowd voice, Stand vpright on thy feete; And he leaped and walked.

text.NRSV: When the crowds saw what Paul had done, they shouted in the Lycaonian language, “The gods have come down to us in human form!”
text.Rotherham: And, the multitudes, seeing what Paul had done, lifted up their voice, in the speech of Lycaonia—The gods, made like unto men, have come down unto us!
text.KJV: 14:11 And when the people saw what Paul had done, they lift vp their voyces, saying in the speech of Lycaonia, The gods are come downe to vs in the likenesse of men.

text.NRSV: Barnabas they called Zeus, and Paul they called Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.
text.Rotherham: And they went on to call Barnabas, Jupiter, and Paul, Mercury, seeing that, he, was the leader of discourse.
text.KJV: 14:12 And they called Barnabas Iupiter, and Paul Mercurius, because hee was the chiefe speaker.

text.NRSV: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was just outside the city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates; he and the crowds wanted to offer sacrifice.
text.Rotherham: Also, the priest of the Jupiter that was before the city, bringing bulls and garlands unto the gates, with the multitudes, would have offered sacrifice.
text.KJV: 14:13 Then the priest of Iupiter, which was before their city, brought oxen, and garlands vnto the gates, and would haue done sacrifice with the people.

text.NRSV: When the apostles Barnabas and Paul heard of it, they tore their clothes and rushed out into the crowd, shouting,
text.Rotherham: But the apostles Barnabas and Paul, hearing of it, rending asunder their own mantles, sprang forward amidst the multitude, crying aloud,
text.KJV: 14:14 Which when the Apostles, Barnabas and Paul heard of, they rent their clothes, and ranne in among the people, crying out,

text.NRSV: “Friends, why are you doing this? We are mortals just like you, and we bring you good news, that you should turn from these worthless things to the living God, who made the heaven and the earth and the sea and all that is in them.
text.Rotherham: and saying—Men! Why, these things, are ye doing? We also, of like nature with you, are men, bringing you the good news, that from these vain things, ye should be turning unto a living God:—Who made heaven and the earth and the sea and all things therein;
text.KJV: 14:15 And saying, Sirs, Why doe yee these things? Wee also are men of like passions with you, and preach vnto you, that ye should turne from these vanities, vnto the liuing God, which made heauen and earth, and the sea, and all things that are therein.

text.NRSV: In past generations he allowed all the nations to follow their own ways;
text.Rotherham: Who in the bygone generations suffered all the nations to be going on in their own ways,—
text.KJV: 14:16 Who in times past, suffred all nations to walke in their owne wayes.

text.NRSV: yet he has not left himself without a witness in doing good—giving you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, and filling you with food and your hearts with joy.”
text.Rotherham: Although, not without witness, he left himself, doing good, from heaven, upon you, giving rain and fruitful seasons, filling, with food and gladness, your hearts.
text.KJV: 14:17 Neuerthelesse, he left not himselfe without witnesse, in that he did good, and gaue vs raine from heauen, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladnesse.

text.NRSV: Even with these words, they scarcely restrained the crowds from offering sacrifice to them.
text.Rotherham: Even these things, saying, scarcely restrained they the multitudes from offering sacrifice unto them.
text.KJV: 14:18 And with these sayings scarse restrained they the people, that they had not done sacrifice vnto them.

text.NRSV: But Jews came there from Antioch and Iconium and won over the crowds. Then they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.
text.Rotherham: But there came thither, from Antioch and Iconium, Jews, and, persuading the multitudes, and stoning Paul, they dragged him outside the city, supposing him to be dead.
text.KJV: 14:19 ¶ And there came thither certaine Iewes from Antioch and Iconium, who perswaded the people, and hauing stoned Paul, drew him out of the citie, supposing he had beene dead.

text.NRSV: But when the disciples surrounded him, he got up and went into the city. The next day he went on with Barnabas to Derbe.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the disciples surrounding him, he rose up, and entered into the city. And, on the morrow, he went forth, with Barnabas, unto Derbe.
text.KJV: 14:20 Howbeit, as the disciples stood round about him, he rose vp, and came into the citie, and the next day he departed with Barnabas to Derbe.

text.NRSV: After they had proclaimed the good news to that city and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, then on to Iconium and Antioch.
text.Rotherham: Delivering the good news unto that city also, and making a good number of disciples, they returned unto Lystra, and unto Iconium, and unto Antioch,—
text.KJV: 14:21 And when they had preached the Gospel to that city, and had taught many, they returned againe to Lystra, and to Iconium, and Antioch,

text.NRSV: There they strengthened the souls of the disciples and encouraged them to continue in the faith, saying, “It is through many persecutions that we must enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: confirming the souls of the disciples, beseeching them to abide in the faith, and [declaring] that, through many tribulations, must we enter into the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: 14:22 Confirming the soules of the disciples, and exhorting them to continue in the faith, aud that we must through much tribulation enter into the kingdome of God.

text.NRSV: And after they had appointed elders for them in each church, with prayer and fasting they entrusted them to the Lord in whom they had come to believe.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, appointing unto them by vote, in each assembly, elders, praying with fastings, they commended them unto the Lord on whom they had believed.
text.KJV: 14:23 And when they had ordeined them Elders in euery Church, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord, on whom they beleeued.

text.NRSV: Then they passed through Pisidia and came to Pamphylia.
text.Rotherham: And, passing through Pisidia, they came into Pamphylia;
text.KJV: 14:24 And after they had passed throughout Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.

text.NRSV: When they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.
text.Rotherham: and, speaking in Perga the word, they came down unto Attalia;
text.KJV: 14:25 And when they had preached the word in Perga, they went downe into Attalia,

text.NRSV: From there they sailed back to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work that they had completed.
text.Rotherham: and, from thence, they set sail for Antioch, whence they had been given up unto the favour of God for the work which they had fulfilled.
text.KJV: 14:26 And thence sailed to Antioch, from whence they had been recommended to the grace of God, for the worke which they fulfilled.

text.NRSV: When they arrived and called the church together, they related all that God had done with them, and how he had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had arrived and gathered together the assembly, they began recounting how many things God had done with them, and that he had opened, unto the nations, a door of faith.
text.KJV: 14:27 And when they were come, and had gathered the Church together, they rehearsed all that God had done with them, and how he had opened the doore of faith vnto the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: And they stayed there with the disciples for some time.
text.Rotherham: And they spent no little time with the disciples.
text.KJV: 14:28 And there they abode long time with the disciples.

text.Base: Then certain individuals came down from Judea and were teaching the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved.”
text.KJV: 15:1 And certaine men which came downe from Iudea, taught the brethren, and said, Except ye be circumcised after the manner of Moses, ye cannot be saued.

text.Base: And after Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and debate with them, Paul and Barnabas and some of the others were appointed to go up to Jerusalem to discuss this question with the apostles and the elders.
text.KJV: 15:2 When therefore Paul and Barnabas had no small dissention and disputation with them, they determined that Paul and Barnabas, and certeine other of them, should goe vp to Hierusalem vnto the Apostles and Elders about this question.

text.Base: So they were sent on their way by the church, and as they passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, they reported the conversion of the Gentiles, and brought great joy to all the believers.
text.KJV: 15:3 And being brought on their way by the Church, they passed thorow Phenice and Samaria, declaring the conuersion of the Gentiles: and they caused great ioy vnto all the brethren.

text.Base: When they came to Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church and the apostles and the elders, and they reported all that God had done with them.
text.KJV: 15:4 And when they were come to Hierusalem, they were receiued of the Church, and of the Apostles, and Elders, and they declared all things that God had done with them.

text.Base: But some believers who belonged to the sect of the Pharisees stood up and said, “It is necessary for them to be circumcised and ordered to keep the law of Moses.”
text.KJV: 15:5 But there rose vp certaine of the sect of the Pharisees which beleeued, saying, that it was needfull to circumcise them, and to cōmand them to keepe the Law of Moses.

text.Base: The apostles and the elders met together to consider this matter.
text.KJV: 15:6 ¶ And the Apostles & Elders came together for to consider of this matter.

text.Base: After there had been much debate, Peter stood up and said to them, “My brothers, you know that in the early days God made a choice among you, that I should be the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the message of the good news and become believers.
text.KJV: 15:7 And when there had bene much disputing, Peter rose vp, and said vnto them, Men and brethren, ye know how that a good while agoe, God made choise among vs, that the Gentiles by my mouth should heare the worde of the Gospel, and beleeue.

text.Base: And God, who knows the human heart, testified to them by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us;
text.KJV: 15:8 And God which knoweth the hearts, bare them witnes, giuing them the holy Ghost, euen as he did vnto vs,

text.Base: and in cleansing their hearts by faith he has made no distinction between them and us.
text.KJV: 15:9 And put no difference between vs & them, purifying their hearts by faith.

text.Base: Now therefore why are you putting God to the test by placing on the neck of the disciples a yoke that neither our ancestors nor we have been able to bear?
text.KJV: 15:10 Now therfore why tempt ye God, to put a yoke vpon the necke of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we were able to beare?

text.Base: On the contrary, we believe that we will be saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, just as they will.”
text.KJV: 15:11 But we beleeue that through the grace of the Lord Iesus Christ, we shal be saued euen as they.

text.Base: The whole assembly kept silence, and listened to Barnabas and Paul as they told of all the signs and wonders that God had done through them among the Gentiles.
text.KJV: 15:12 ¶ Then all the multitude kept silence, and gaue audience to Barnabas and Paul, declaring what miracles and wonders God had wrought among the Gentiles by them.

text.Base: After they finished speaking, James replied, “My brothers, listen to me.
text.KJV: 15:13 ¶ And after they had helde their peace, Iames answered, saying, Men and brethren, hearken vnto me.

text.Base: Simeon has related how God first looked favorably on the Gentiles, to take from among them a people for his name.
text.KJV: 15:14 Simeon hath declared how God at the first did visite the Gentiles to take out of them a people for his Name.

text.Base: This agrees with the words of the prophets, as it is written,
text.KJV: 15:15 And to this agree the words of the Prophets, as it is written,

text.Base: ‘After this I will return, and I will rebuild the dwelling of David, which has fallen; from its ruins I will rebuild it, and I will set it up,
text.KJV: 15:16 After this I will returne, and wil build againe the Tabernacle of Dauid, which is fallen downe: and I will build againe the ruines thereof, and I will set it vp:

text.Base: so that all other peoples may seek the Lord— even all the Gentiles over whom my name has been called. Thus says the Lord, who has been making these things known from of old.’
text.KJV: 15:17 That the residue of men might seeke after the Lord, and all the Gentiles, vpon whom my Name is called, sayth the Lord, who doeth all these things.

text.Base: Therefore I have reached the decision that we should not trouble those Gentiles who are turning to God,
text.KJV: 15:19 Wherefore my sentence is, that we trouble not them, which from among the Gentiles are turned to God:

text.Base: but we should write to them to abstain only from things polluted by idols and from fornication and from whatever has been strangled and from blood.
text.KJV: 15:20 But that wee write vnto them, that they abstaine from pollutions of Idoles, and from fornication, and from things strangled, and from blood.

text.Base: For in every city, for generations past, Moses has had those who proclaim him, for he has been read aloud every sabbath in the synagogues.”
text.KJV: 15:21 For Moses of olde time hath in euery citie them that preach him, being read in the Synagogues euery Sabbath day.

text.Base: Then the apostles and the elders, with the consent of the whole church, decided to choose men from among their members and to send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. They sent Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas, leaders among the brothers,
text.KJV: 15:22 Then pleased it the Apostles and Elders with the whole Church, to send chosen men of their owne company to Antioch, with Paul and Barnabas: namely, Iudas surnamed Barsabas, & Silas, chiefe men among the brethren,

text.Base: with the following letter: “The apostles and the elders, your brothers, To the believers of Gentile origin in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia, greetings.
text.KJV: 15:23 And wrote letters by them after this maner, The Apostles and Elders, and brethren, send greeting vnto the brethren, which are of the Gentiles in Antioch, and Syria, and Cilicia.

text.Base: Since we have heard that certain persons who have gone out from us, though with no instructions from us, have said things to disturb you and have unsettled your minds,
text.KJV: 15:24 Forasmuch as we haue heard, that certaine which went out from vs, haue troubled you with words, subuerting your soules, saying, Ye must be circumcised, and keepe the Law, to whom we gaue no such commandement:

text.Base: we have decided unanimously to choose representatives and send them to you, along with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
text.KJV: 15:25 It seemed good vnto vs, being assembled with one accord, to send chosen men vnto you, with our beloued Barnabas and Paul,

text.Base: who have risked their lives for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 15:26 Men that haue hazarded their liues for the Name of our Lord Iesus Christ.

text.Base: We have therefore sent Judas and Silas, who themselves will tell you the same things by word of mouth.
text.KJV: 15:27 Wee haue sent therefore Iudas and Silas, who shall also tell you the same things by mouth.

text.Base: For it has seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us to impose on you no further burden than these essentials:
text.KJV: 15:28 For it seemed good to the holy Ghost, and to vs, to lay vpon you no greater burden then these necessarie things;

text.Base: that you abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what is strangled and from fornication. If you keep yourselves from these, you will do well. Farewell.”
text.KJV: 15:29 That ye abstaine from meates offered to idoles, and from blood, & from things strangled, and from fornication: from which if ye keepe your selues, yee shall doe well. Fare ye well.

text.Base: So they were sent off and went down to Antioch. When they gathered the congregation together, they delivered the letter.
text.KJV: 15:30 So when they were dismissed, they came to Antioch: and when they had gathered the multitude together, they deliuered the Epistle.

text.Base: When its members read it, they rejoiced at the exhortation.
text.KJV: 15:31 Which when they had read, they reioyced for the consolation.

text.Base: Judas and Silas, who were themselves prophets, said much to encourage and strengthen the believers.
text.KJV: 15:32 And Iudas and Silas, being Prophets also themselues, exhorted the brethren with many words, and confirmed them:

text.Base: After they had been there for some time, they were sent off in peace by the believers to those who had sent them.
text.KJV: 15:33 And after they had taried there a space, they were let goe in peace from the breehren vnto the Apostles.

text.Base: But Paul and Barnabas remained in Antioch, and there, with many others, they taught and proclaimed the word of the Lord.
text.KJV: 15:35 Paul also and Barnabas continued in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.

text.Base: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Come, let us return and visit the believers in every city where we proclaimed the word of the Lord and see how they are doing.”
text.KJV: 15:36 ¶ And some dayes after, Paul said vnto Barnabas, Let vs go againe and visit our brethren, in euery city where we haue preached the word of the Lord, and see how they doe.

text.Base: Barnabas wanted to take with them John called Mark.
text.KJV: 15:37 And Barnabas determined to take with them Iohn, whose surname was Marke.

text.Base: But Paul decided not to take with them one who had deserted them in Pamphylia and had not accompanied them in the work.
text.KJV: 15:38 But Paul thought not good to take him with them; who departed from them from Pamphylia, and went not with them to the worke.

text.Base: The disagreement was so sharp that they parted company; Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus.
text.KJV: 15:39 And the contention was so sharpe betweene them, that they departed asunder one from the other: & so Barnabas tooke Marke, & sailed vnto Cyprus.

text.Base: But Paul chose Silas and set out, the believers commending him to the grace of the Lord.
text.KJV: 15:40 And Paul chose Silas, and departed, being recommended by the brethren vnto the grace of God.

text.Base: He went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
text.KJV: 15:41 And he went thorow Syria and Cilicia, confirming the Churches.

text.NRSV: Paul went on also to Derbe and to Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy, the son of a Jewish woman who was a believer; but his father was a Greek.
text.Rotherham: And he came even unto Derbe, and unto Lystra; and lo! a certain disciple, was there, by name Timothy, son of a believing Jewish woman, but whose father was a Greek,—
text.KJV: 16:1 Then came he to Derbe, and Lystra: and behold, a certaine disciple was there, named Timotheus, the son of a certaine woman which was a Iewesse, and beleeued: but his father was a Greeke:

text.NRSV: He was well spoken of by the believers in Lystra and Iconium.
text.Rotherham: who was well-attested by the brethren in Lystra, and Iconium.
text.KJV: 16:2 Which was well reported of by the brethren that were at Lystra and Iconium.

text.NRSV: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him; and he took him and had him circumcised because of the Jews who were in those places, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.
text.Rotherham: The same, would Paul have go forth with him, and took and circumcised him, on account of the Jews who were in those places; for they one and all knew that, his father, was, a Greek.
text.KJV: 16:3 Him would Paul haue to go forth with him, and tooke, and circumcised him, because of the Iewes which were in those quarters: for they knew all, that his father was a Greeke.

text.NRSV: As they went from town to town, they delivered to them for observance the decisions that had been reached by the apostles and elders who were in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, as they passed through the cities, they were delivering unto them, for observance, the decrees which had been decided upon by the Apostles and Elders who were in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 16:4 And as they went through the cities, they deliuered them the decrees for to keepe, that were ordeined of the Apostles and Elders, which were at Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and increased in numbers daily.
text.Rotherham: The assemblies, therefore, were being confirmed in the faith, and increasing in number, every day.
text.KJV: 16:5 And so were the Churches established in the faith, and increased in number dayly.

text.NRSV: They went through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.
text.Rotherham: And they passed through the Phrygian and Galatian country, being forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia;
text.KJV: 16:6 Now when they had gone thorowout Phrygia, and the region of Galatia, and were forbidden of the holy Ghost to preach the word in Asia,

text.NRSV: When they had come opposite Mysia, they attempted to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them;
text.Rotherham: but, coming along Mysia, they were attempting to journey into Bithynia,—and, the Spirit of Jesus, suffered them not;
text.KJV: 16:7 After they were come to Mysia, they assayed to goe into Bithynia: but the Spirit suffered them not.

text.NRSV: so, passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas.
text.Rotherham: but, passing by Mysia, they came down unto Troas.
text.KJV: 16:8 And they passing by Mysia, came downe to Troas.

text.NRSV: During the night a vision appeared to Paul: a man of Macedonia was standing there and pleading with him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us.”
text.Rotherham: And, a vision, by night, unto Paul appeared:—A man of Macedonia, there was, standing and beseeching him, and saying—Come over into Macedonia, and bring us succour!
text.KJV: 16:9 And a vision appeared to Paul in the night: There stood a man of Macedonia, and prayed him, saying, Come ouer into Macedonia, and helpe vs.

text.NRSV: When he had seen the vision, we immediately tried to cross over to Macedonia, being convinced that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
text.Rotherham: Now, when, the vision, he had seen, straightway, we sought to go forth unto Macedonia, concluding that God, had summoned us to tell the glad tidings unto them.
text.KJV: 16:10 And after he had seene the vision, immediatly we endeuoured to goe into Macedonia, assuredly gathering, that the Lord had called vs for to preach the Gospel vnto them.

text.NRSV: We set sail from Troas and took a straight course to Samothrace, the next day to Neapolis,
text.Rotherham: Setting sail, therefore, from Troas, we ran straight into Samothracia, and, on the morrow, unto New City,
text.KJV: 16:11 Therfore loosing from Troas, we came with a straight course to Samothracia, and the next day to Neapolis:

text.NRSV: and from there to Philippi, which is a leading city of the district of Macedonia and a Roman colony. We remained in this city for some days.
text.Rotherham: and, from thence, unto Philippi,—which, indeed, is the first city of the part of Macedonia—a colony. And we were, in this city, spending certain days;
text.KJV: 16:12 And from thence to Philippi, which is the chiefe citie of that part of Macedonia, and a Colonie: and we were in that citie abiding certaine dayes.

text.NRSV: On the sabbath day we went outside the gate by the river, where we supposed there was a place of prayer; and we sat down and spoke to the women who had gathered there.
text.Rotherham: and on the day of rest, we went forth outside the gate, beside a river, where we supposed there was a place of prayer,—and, sitting down, we went on to speak unto the women who had come together.
text.KJV: 16:13 And on the Sabboth we went out of the citie by a riuer side, where prayer was wont to be made, & we sate downe, and spake vnto the women which resorted thither.

text.NRSV: A certain woman named Lydia, a worshiper of God, was listening to us; she was from the city of Thyatira and a dealer in purple cloth. The Lord opened her heart to listen eagerly to what was said by Paul.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain woman, by name Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, devout towards God, was hearkening, whose heart, the Lord, fully opened, to be giving heed unto the things being spoken by Paul.
text.KJV: 16:14 ¶ And a certaine woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the citie of Thyatira, which worshipped God, heard vs: whose heart the Lord opened, that she attended vnto the things which were spoken of Paul.

text.NRSV: When she and her household were baptized, she urged us, saying, “If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come and stay at my home.” And she prevailed upon us.
text.Rotherham: And, when she was immersed, and her house, she besought [us], saying—If ye have judged me to be a believer in the Lord, come into my house, and abide [there] . And she constrained us.
text.KJV: 16:15 And when she was baptized, and her houshold, she besought vs, saying, If ye haue iudged me to bee faithfull to the Lord, come into my house, and abide there. And she constrained vs.

text.NRSV: One day, as we were going to the place of prayer, we met a slave-girl who had a spirit of divination and brought her owners a great deal of money by fortune-telling.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as we were on our way unto the place of prayer, a certain damsel, having a spirit of Python, met us,—who, indeed, much gain, was presenting unto her masters, by divining.
text.KJV: 16:16 ¶ And it came to passe, as we went to prayer, a certaine Damosell possessed with a spirit of diuination, met vs: which brought her masters much gaine by soothsaying.

text.NRSV: While she followed Paul and us, she would cry out, “These men are slaves of the Most High God, who proclaim to you a way of salvation.”
text.Rotherham: The same, following after Paul and us, kept crying aloud, saying—These men, are servants of the Most High God,—who, indeed, are declaring unto you a way of salvation.
text.KJV: 16:17 The same followed Paul and vs, and cried, saying, These men are the seruants of the most hie God, which shew vnto vs the way of saluation.

text.NRSV: She kept doing this for many days. But Paul, very much annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I order you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her.” And it came out that very hour.
text.Rotherham: And, this, she continued to do for many days. But Paul, worn out, and turning unto the spirit, said—I charge thee, in the name of Jesus Christ, to come out from her. And it came out the same hour.
text.KJV: 16:18 And this did she many dayes: but Paul being grieued, turned and said to the spirit, I command thee in the Name of Iesus Christ, to come out of her. And he came out the same houre.

text.NRSV: But when her owners saw that their hope of making money was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the authorities.
text.Rotherham: And, her masters, seeing that their hope of gain had gone out, laying hold on Paul and Silas, dragged them into the market-place, unto the rulers;
text.KJV: 16:19 ¶ And when her Masters saw that the hope of their gaines was gone, they caught Paul and Silas, and drew them into the market place, vnto the rulers,

text.NRSV: When they had brought them before the magistrates, they said, “These men are disturbing our city; they are Jews
text.Rotherham: and, leading them forward unto the magistrates, said—These men, are exceedingly troubling our city, they, being Jews,
text.KJV: 16:20 And brought them to the Magistrates, saying, These men being Iewes, do exceedingly trouble our city,

text.NRSV: and are advocating customs that are not lawful for us as Romans to adopt or observe.”
text.Rotherham: And are declaring customs, which it is not allowable for us either to accept or to observe, being Romans.
text.KJV: 16:21 And teach customes which are not lawfull for vs to receiue, neither to obserue, being Romanes.

text.NRSV: The crowd joined in attacking them, and the magistrates had them stripped of their clothing and ordered them to be beaten with rods.
text.Rotherham: And the multitude rose up together against them, and, the magistrates, rending off them their mantles, were giving orders to beat them with rods;
text.KJV: 16:22 And the multitude rose vp together against them, and the Magistrates rent off their clothes, and commanded to beate them.

text.NRSV: After they had given them a severe flogging, they threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to keep them safely.
text.Rotherham: and, laying upon them many stripes, they thrust them into prison, charging the prison-keeper, safely, to be keeping them:
text.KJV: 16:23 And when they had layed many stripes vpon them, they cast them into prison, charging the Iaylour to keepe them safely.

text.NRSV: Following these instructions, he put them in the innermost cell and fastened their feet in the stocks.
text.Rotherham: who, a charge like this receiving, thrust them into the inner prison, and, their feet, made he fast in the stocks.
text.KJV: 16:24 Who hauing receiued such a charge, thrust them into the inner prison, & made their feet fast in the stockes.

text.NRSV: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them.
text.Rotherham: And, at midnight, Paul and Silas, being at prayer, began singing praise unto God; and the prisoners unto them, did hearken.
text.KJV: 16:25 ¶ And at midnight, Paul and Silas prayed, and sang praises vnto God: and the prisoners heard them.

text.NRSV: Suddenly there was an earthquake, so violent that the foundations of the prison were shaken; and immediately all the doors were opened and everyone’s chains were unfastened.
text.Rotherham: And, suddenly, a great earthquake took place,—so that the foundations of the prison were shaken, and all the doors were [instantly] set open, and the bonds of all were unfastened.
text.KJV: 16:26 And suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken: and immediately all the doores were opened, and euery ones bands were loosed.

text.NRSV: When the jailer woke up and saw the prison doors wide open, he drew his sword and was about to kill himself, since he supposed that the prisoners had escaped.
text.Rotherham: And, the prison-keeper, being wakened and seeing that the doors of the prison had been opened, drawing his sword, was about to kill himself, supposing the prisoners to have fled.
text.KJV: 16:27 And the keeper of the prison awaking out of his sleepe, and seeing the prison doores open, he drew out his sword, and would haue killed himselfe, supposing that the prisoners had beene fled.

text.NRSV: But Paul shouted in a loud voice, “Do not harm yourself, for we are all here.”
text.Rotherham: But Paul called out with a loud voice, saying—By no means, do thyself harm, for we are, one and all, here.
text.KJV: 16:28 But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, Doe thy selfe no harme, for we are all heere.

text.NRSV: The jailer called for lights, and rushing in, he fell down trembling before Paul and Silas.
text.Rotherham: And, asking for a light, he sprang in, and becoming, agitated, fell down unto Paul and Silas,
text.KJV: 16:29 Then hee called for a light, and sprang in, and came trembling, and fell downe before Paul and Silas,

text.NRSV: Then he brought them outside and said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”
text.Rotherham: and, leading them forth outside, said—Sirs! what must I be doing, that I may be saved?
text.KJV: 16:30 And brought them out, and said, Sirs, what must I doe to be saued?

text.NRSV: They answered, “Believe on the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Believe on the Lord Jesus, and thou shalt be saved, thou, and thy house.
text.KJV: 16:31 And they saide, Beleeue on the Lord Iesus Christ, and thou shalt be saued, and thy house.

text.NRSV: They spoke the word of the Lord to him and to all who were in his house.
text.Rotherham: And they spake unto him the word of God, with all who were in his house.
text.KJV: 16:32 And they spake vnto him the word of the Lord, and to all that were in his house.

text.NRSV: At the same hour of the night he took them and washed their wounds; then he and his entire household were baptized without delay.
text.Rotherham: And, taking them with him, in that hour of the night, he bathed them from their stripes, and was immersed, he, and his, one and all, on the spot;
text.KJV: 16:33 And hee tooke them the same houre of the night, and washed their stripes, and was baptized, hee and all his, straightway.

text.NRSV: He brought them up into the house and set food before them; and he and his entire household rejoiced that he had become a believer in God.
text.Rotherham: and, leading them up into his house, he set near a table,—and exulted, having, with all his house, believed in God.
text.KJV: 16:34 And when he had brought them into his house, hee set meat before them, and reioyced, beleeuing in God with all his house.

text.NRSV: When morning came, the magistrates sent the police, saying, “Let those men go.”
text.Rotherham: And. when day came. the magistrates sent off the constables saying—Let those men go!
text.KJV: 16:35 And when it was day, the Magistrates sent the Sergeants, saying, Let those men goe.

text.NRSV: And the jailer reported the message to Paul, saying, “The magistrates sent word to let you go; therefore come out now and go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: And the prison-keeper reported the words unto Paul—The magistrates have sent, that ye be let go. Now, therefore, going forth, be taking your journey in peace.
text.KJV: 16:36 And the keeper of the prison told this saying to Paul, The Magistrates haue sent to let you goe: Now therefore depart, and goe in peace.

text.NRSV: But Paul replied, “They have beaten us in public, uncondemned, men who are Roman citizens, and have thrown us into prison; and now are they going to discharge us in secret? Certainly not! Let them come themselves and take us out.”
text.Rotherham: But, Paul, said unto them—Beating us, in public, uncondemned, men that are Romans, they thrust us into prison;—and, now, by stealth, are they thrusting us forth? Nay, verily! but let them come, themselves, and lead us out!
text.KJV: 16:37 But Paul said vnto them, They haue beaten vs openly vncondemned, being Romanes, and haue cast vs into prison, and now doe they thrust vs out priuily? Nay verily, but let them come themselues, and fetch vs out.

text.NRSV: The police reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Roman citizens;
text.Rotherham: And the constables reported unto the magistrates these words; and they were struck with fear, when they heard that they were, Romans;
text.KJV: 16:38 And the Sergeants tolde these words vnto the Magistrates: and they feared when they heard that they were Romanes.

text.NRSV: so they came and apologized to them. And they took them out and asked them to leave the city.
text.Rotherham: and came, and besought them, and, leading them out, went on to request them to depart from the city.
text.KJV: 16:39 And they came and besought them, and brought them out, and desired them to depart out of the citie.

text.NRSV: After leaving the prison they went to Lydia’s home; and when they had seen and encouraged the brothers and sisters there, they departed.
text.Rotherham: And so, coming forth from the prison, they went unto Lydia, and, seeing the brethren, they comforted them, and went forth.
text.KJV: 16:40 And they went out of the prison, and entred into the house of Lydia, and when they had seene the brethren, they comforted them, and departed.

text.NRSV: After they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: And, travelling through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Jews;
text.KJV: 17:1 Now when they had passed thorow Amphipolis, and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Iewes.

text.NRSV: And Paul went in, as was his custom, and on three sabbath days he argued with them from the scriptures,
text.Rotherham: and, according to Paul’s custom, he went in unto them, and, for three sabbaths, reasoned with them from the Scriptures,—
text.KJV: 17:2 And Paul, as his maner was, went in vnto them, and three Sabbath dayes reasoned with them out of the Scriptures,

text.NRSV: explaining and proving that it was necessary for the Messiah to suffer and to rise from the dead, and saying, “This is the Messiah, Jesus whom I am proclaiming to you.”
text.Rotherham: opening up, and setting forth, that it was needful for, the Christ, to suffer, and to arise from among the dead; and [saying], This, is the Christ,—Jesus, whom, I, am declaring unto you.
text.KJV: 17:3 Opening and alleadging, that Christ must needs haue suffered and risen againe from the dead: and that this Iesus whom I preach vnto you, is Christ.

text.NRSV: Some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, as did a great many of the devout Greeks and not a few of the leading women.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were persuaded, and cast in their lot with Paul and Silas; also, of the devout Greeks, a great throng, and, of the chief women, not a few.
text.KJV: 17:4 And some of them beleeued, and consorted with Paul and Silas: and of the deuout Greekes a great multitude, and of the chiefe women not a few.

text.NRSV: But the Jews became jealous, and with the help of some ruffians in the marketplaces they formed a mob and set the city in an uproar. While they were searching for Paul and Silas to bring them out to the assembly, they attacked Jason’s house.
text.Rotherham: But the Jews, being, jealous, and taking unto themselves certain wicked men, of the rabble, and making a riot, were setting the city in an uproar; and, besieging the house of Jason, were seeking to lead them forth unto the populace,—
text.KJV: 17:5 ¶ But the Iewes which beleeued not, mooued with enuie, tooke vnto them certaine lewd fellowes of the baser sort, and gathered a company, and set all the citie on an vprore, and assaulted the house of Iason, and sought to bring them out to the people.

text.NRSV: When they could not find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city authorities, shouting, “These people who have been turning the world upside down have come here also,
text.Rotherham: and, not finding them, they began dragging Jason and certain brethren unto the city-rulers, shouting—They who have thrown the inhabited earth into confusion, the same, hither also, are come,—
text.KJV: 17:6 And when they found them not, they drew Iason, and certaine brethren vnto the rulers of the citie, crying, These that haue turned the world vpside downe, are come hither also,

text.NRSV: and Jason has entertained them as guests. They are all acting contrary to the decrees of the emperor, saying that there is another king named Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: unto whom Jason hath given welcome; and, these all, contrary to the decrees of Caesar, are acting,—saying that there is another king, Jesus.
text.KJV: 17:7 Whom Iason hath receiued: and these all doe contrary to the decrees of Cesar, saying, that there is another King, one Iesus.

text.NRSV: The people and the city officials were disturbed when they heard this,
text.Rotherham: And they troubled the multitude and the city-rulers, when they heard these things;
text.KJV: 17:8 And they troubled the people, and the rulers of the citie, when they heard these things.

text.NRSV: and after they had taken bail from Jason and the others, they let them go.
text.Rotherham: and, taking security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.
text.KJV: 17:9 And when they had taken securitie of Iason, and of the other, they let them goe.

text.NRSV: That very night the believers sent Paul and Silas off to Beroea; and when they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
text.Rotherham: But, the brethren, straightway, during the night, sent away both Paul and Silas unto Beroea, who, indeed, arriving, unto the synagogue of the Jews, went off;
text.KJV: 17:10 ¶ And the brethren immediatly sent away Paul and Silas by night vnto Berea: who comming thither, went into the Synagogue of the Iewes.

text.NRSV: These Jews were more receptive than those in Thessalonica, for they welcomed the message with very great eagerness and examined the scriptures every day to see whether these things were so.
text.Rotherham: and, these, were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they welcomed the word with all readiness of mind, daily, searching the Scriptures,—whether these things could be so.
text.KJV: 17:11 These were more noble then those in Thessalonica, in that they receiued the word with all readinesse of minde, and searched the Scriptures dayly, whether those things were so.

text.NRSV: Many of them therefore believed, including not a few Greek women and men of high standing.
text.Rotherham: Many, therefore, from among them, believed, and, of the Grecian women of the higher class, and of men, not a few.
text.KJV: 17:12 Therefore many of them beleeued: also of honourable women which were Greekes, and of men not a few.

text.NRSV: But when the Jews of Thessalonica learned that the word of God had been proclaimed by Paul in Beroea as well, they came there too, to stir up and incite the crowds.
text.Rotherham: But, when the Jews from Thessalonica came to know that, in Beroea also, had the word of God been declared by Paul, they came thither also, stirring up and troubling the multitudes.
text.KJV: 17:13 But when the Iewes of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was preached of Paul at Berea, they came thither also, and stirred vp the people.

text.NRSV: Then the believers immediately sent Paul away to the coast, but Silas and Timothy remained behind.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, then, immediately, the brethren sent away, Paul, to be journeying as far as unto the sea; and both Silas and Timothy stayed behind, there.
text.KJV: 17:14 And then immediatly the brethren sent away Paul, to goe as it were to the sea: but Silas and Timotheus abode there still.

text.NRSV: Those who conducted Paul brought him as far as Athens; and after receiving instructions to have Silas and Timothy join him as soon as possible, they left him.
text.Rotherham: But, they who were conducting Paul, brought him as far as Athens, and, receiving a commandment unto Silas and Timothy, that with, all possible speed, they would come unto him, they departed.
text.KJV: 17:15 And they that conducted Paul, brought him vnto Athens, and receiuing a commaundement vnto Silas and Timotheus, for to come to him with all speed, they departed.

text.NRSV: While Paul was waiting for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to see that the city was full of idols.
text.Rotherham: But, while, in Athens, Paul was expecting them, his spirit within him was being urged on, seeing how the city was given to idols.
text.KJV: 17:16 ¶ Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was stirred in him, when hee saw the city wholy giuen to idolatrie.

text.NRSV: So he argued in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and also in the marketplace every day with those who happened to be there.
text.Rotherham: So then, he began reasoning in the synagogue with the Jews, and with them who worshipped; and, in the market-place, every day, with them who happened to be at hand.
text.KJV: 17:17 Therefore disputed he in the Synagogue with the Iewes, and with the deuout persons, and in the market dayly with them that met with him.

text.NRSV: Also some Epicurean and Stoic philosophers debated with him. Some said, “What does this babbler want to say?” Others said, “He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign deities.” (This was because he was telling the good news about Jesus and the resurrection.)
text.Rotherham: But, certain both of the Epicurean and of the Stoic philosophers, were encountering him; and some were saying—What might this picker-up-of-scraps wish to be saying? And, others—Of foreign demons, he seemeth to be a declarer: because, of Jesus and the Resurrection, he was announcing the joyful tidings.
text.KJV: 17:18 Then certaine Philosophers of the Epicureans, and of the Stoikes, encountred him: and some said, What will this babbler say? Othersome, He seemeth to be a setter foorth of strange gods: because hee preached vnto them Iesus, and the resurrection.

text.NRSV: So they took him and brought him to the Areopagus and asked, “May we know what this new teaching is that you are presenting?
text.Rotherham: And so, laying hold of him, they brought him up, to the Hill of Mars, saying—Can we get to know what this new teaching is, which, by thee, is being spoken.
text.KJV: 17:19 And they tooke him, and brought him vnto Areopagus, saying, May we know what this new doctrine, whereof thou speakest, is?

text.NRSV: It sounds rather strange to us, and we would like to know what it means.”
text.Rotherham: For, certain foreign things, art thou bringing into our hearing: We are minded to get to know, therefore, what these things please to be!
text.KJV: 17:20 For thou bringest certaine strange things to our eares: we would know therefore what these things meane.

text.NRSV: Now all the Athenians and the foreigners living there would spend their time in nothing but telling or hearing something new.
text.Rotherham: Now, all Athenians and the sojourning foreigners, unto nothing else, were devoting their leisure, than to be telling or hearing, something newer.
text.KJV: 17:21 (For all the Athenians and strangers which were there, spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell or to heare some new thing.)

text.NRSV: Then Paul stood in front of the Areopagus and said, “Athenians, I see how extremely religious you are in every way.
text.Rotherham: And Paul taking his stand in the midst of the Hill of Mars, said—Ye men of Athens! In every way, how unusually reverent of the demons ye are, I perceive.
text.KJV: 17:22 ¶ Then Paul stood in the mids of Mars-hill, and said, Yee men of Athens, I perceiue that in all things yee are too superstitious.

text.NRSV: For as I went through the city and looked carefully at the objects of your worship, I found among them an altar with the inscription, ‘To an unknown god.’ What therefore you worship as unknown, this I proclaim to you.
text.Rotherham: For, passing through, and carefully observing your objects of devotion, I found an altar also, in which was inscribed—Unto an Unknown God. What, therefore, not knowing, ye reverence, the same, do, I, declare unto you.
text.KJV: 17:23 For as I passed by, and beheld your deuotions, I found an Altar with this inscription, TO THE VNKNOWEN GOD. Whom therefore yee ignorantly worship, him declare I vnto you.

text.NRSV: The God who made the world and everything in it, he who is Lord of heaven and earth, does not live in shrines made by human hands,
text.Rotherham: The God that made the world and all things that are therein, the same, being, Lord, of heaven and earth, not in hand-made shrines, doth dwell,
text.KJV: 17:24 God that made the world, and all things therein, seeing that hee is Lord of heauen and earth, dwelleth not in Temples made with hands:

text.NRSV: nor is he served by human hands, as though he needed anything, since he himself gives to all mortals life and breath and all things.
text.Rotherham: nor, by human hands, is waited upon, as though in want of anything, himself, giving unto all life and breath and all things;
text.KJV: 17:25 Neither is worshipped with mens hands as though he needed any thing, seeing hee giueth to all, life and breath, and all things,

text.NRSV: From one ancestor he made all nations to inhabit the whole earth, and he allotted the times of their existence and the boundaries of the places where they would live,
text.Rotherham: he made also, of one, every nation of men to dwell upon all the face of the earth,—marking out fitting opportunities, and the bounds of their dwelling place,
text.KJV: 17:26 And hath made of one blood all nations of men, for to dwell on all the face of the earth, and hath determined the times before appointed, and the bounds of their habitation:

text.NRSV: so that they would search for God and perhaps grope for him and find him—though indeed he is not far from each one of us.
text.Rotherham: that they might be seeking God—if, after all, indeed, they might feel after him and find him,—although, in truth, he is already not far from any one of us.
text.KJV: 17:27 That they should seeke the Lord, if haply they might feele after him and finde him, though he be not farre from euery one of vs.

text.NRSV: For ‘In him we live and move and have our being’; as even some of your own poets have said, ‘For we too are his offspring.’
text.Rotherham: For, in him, we live and move and are: as, even some of your own poets, have said—For, his offspring also, we are.
text.KJV: 17:28 For in him we liue, and mooue, and haue our being, as certaine also of your owne Poets haue said, For we are also his offspring.

text.NRSV: Since we are God’s offspring, we ought not to think that the deity is like gold, or silver, or stone, an image formed by the art and imagination of mortals.
text.Rotherham: Being, then, offspring, of God, we ought not to be supposing that, unto gold or silver or stone, graven by art and device of man, the Divine, is like.
text.KJV: 17:29 Forasmuch then as wee are the offspring of God, wee ought not to thinke that the Godhead is like vnto golde, or siluer, or stone grauen by arte, and mans deuice.

text.NRSV: While God has overlooked the times of human ignorance, now he commands all people everywhere to repent,
text.Rotherham: The times of ignorance, therefore, overlooking, God, as things now are, is charging all men everywhere to repent,
text.KJV: 17:30 And the times of this ignorance God winked at, but now commandeth all men euery where to repent:

text.NRSV: because he has fixed a day on which he will have the world judged in righteousness by a man whom he has appointed, and of this he has given assurance to all by raising him from the dead.”
text.Rotherham: inasmuch as he hath appointed a day, in which he is about to be judging the habitable earth in righteousness, by a man whom he hath pointed out,—offering faith unto all, by raising him from among the dead?
text.KJV: 17:31 Because hee hath appointed a day in the which he will iudge the world in righteousnesse, by that man whom hee hath ordeined, whereof he hath giuen assurance vnto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead.

text.NRSV: When they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some scoffed; but others said, “We will hear you again about this.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when they heard of raising the dead, some, indeed, began to mock, while, others, said—We will hear thee, concerning this, even again.
text.KJV: 17:32 ¶ And when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked: and others said, Wee will heare thee againe of this matter.

text.NRSV: At that point Paul left them.
text.Rotherham: Thus, Paul, came forth out of their midst.
text.KJV: 17:33 So Paul departed from among them.

text.NRSV: But some of them joined him and became believers, including Dionysius the Areopagite and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
text.Rotherham: But, certain persons, joining themselves unto him, believed; among whom were even Dionysius the Mars-hill judge, and a woman by name Damaris, and others with them.
text.KJV: 17:34 Howbeit, certaine men claue vnto him, and beleeued: among the which was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.

text.NRSV: After this Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
text.Rotherham: After these things, withdrawing from Athens, he came unto Corinth;
text.KJV: 18:1 After these things, Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth,

text.NRSV: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul went to see them,
text.Rotherham: and, finding a certain Jew, by name Aquila, of Pontus by birth,—lately come from Italy, and Priscilla his wife, because Claudius had ordered all the Jews to be leaving Rome, he came unto them,
text.KJV: 18:2 And found a certaine Iewe named Aquila, borne in Pontus, lately come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla, (because that Claudius had commanded all Iewes to depart from Rome) and came vnto them.

text.NRSV: and, because he was of the same trade, he stayed with them, and they worked together—by trade they were tentmakers.
text.Rotherham: and, because he was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought, for they were tent-makers by their trade.
text.KJV: 18:3 And because hee was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought (for by their occupation they were tentmakers.)

text.NRSV: Every sabbath he would argue in the synagogue and would try to convince Jews and Greeks.
text.Rotherham: And he began reasoning in the synagogue every sabbath, and was persuading both Jews and Greeks.
text.KJV: 18:4 And hee reasoned in the Synagogue euery Sabbath, and perswaded the Iewes, and the Greekes.

text.NRSV: When Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul was occupied with proclaiming the word, testifying to the Jews that the Messiah was Jesus.
text.Rotherham: When, however, both Silas and Timothy had come down from Macedonia, Paul began to be urged on in the word, bearing full witness unto the Jews that, Jesus, was, the Christ.
text.KJV: 18:5 And when Silas and Timotheus were come from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in spirit, and testified to the Iewes, that Iesus was Christ.

text.NRSV: When they opposed and reviled him, in protest he shook the dust from his clothes and said to them, “Your blood be on your own heads! I am innocent. From now on I will go to the Gentiles.”
text.Rotherham: But, as they began opposing and defaming, shaking out his garments, he said unto them—Your blood, be upon your own head! Pure, am, I: henceforth, unto the nations, will I go.
text.KJV: 18:6 And when they opposed themselues, and blasphemed, he shooke his raiment, and said vnto them, Your blood be vpon your owne heads, I am cleane: from henceforth I will goe vnto the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: Then he left the synagogue and went to the house of a man named Titius Justus, a worshiper of God; his house was next door to the synagogue.
text.Rotherham: And, removing from thence, he came into the house of a certain man by name Titius Justus, who worshipped God, whose house was adjoining unto the synagogue.
text.KJV: 18:7 ¶ And hee departed thence, and entred into a certaine mans house, named Iustus, one that worshipped God, whose house ioyned hard to the Synagogue.

text.NRSV: Crispus, the official of the synagogue, became a believer in the Lord, together with all his household; and many of the Corinthians who heard Paul became believers and were baptized.
text.Rotherham: But, Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord, with all his house. And, many of the Corinthians, hearing, were believing, and being immersed.
text.KJV: 18:8 And Crispus, the chiefe ruler of the Synagogue, beleeued on the Lord, with all his house: and many of the Corinthians, hearing, beleeued, and were baptized.

text.NRSV: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, “Do not be afraid, but speak and do not be silent;
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said by night, through means of a vision, unto Paul—Be not afraid! but be speaking,—and do not hold thy peace;
text.KJV: 18:9 Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision, Be not afraid, but speake, and holde not thy peace:

text.NRSV: for I am with you, and no one will lay a hand on you to harm you, for there are many in this city who are my people.”
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, I, am with thee, and, no one, shall set upon thee to harm thee; inasmuch as I have much people in this city.
text.KJV: 18:10 For I am with thee, and no man shal set on thee, to hurt thee: for I haue much people in this city.

text.NRSV: He stayed there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.
text.Rotherham: And he remained a year and six months, teaching among them the word of God.
text.KJV: 18:11 And hee continued there a yeere and sixe monethes, teaching the word of God among them.

text.NRSV: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews made a united attack on Paul and brought him before the tribunal.
text.Rotherham: But, when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews, with one accord, set upon Paul, and led him unto the judgment-seat,
text.KJV: 18:12 ¶ And when Gallio was the Deputie of Achaia, the Iewes made insurrection with one accord against Paul, and brought him to the iudgement seat,

text.NRSV: They said, “This man is persuading people to worship God in ways that are contrary to the law.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Contrary to the law, is this one seducing men to be worshipping God.
text.KJV: 18:13 Saying, This fellow perswadeth men to worship God contrary to the Law.
