text.NRSV: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, “If it were a matter of misfortune or a villainous crime, I would be justified in accepting the complaint of you Jews;
text.Rotherham: But, as Paul was about to open his mouth, Gallio said unto the Jews—If, indeed, it had been some wrong or wicked recklessness, O Jews, with reason, in that case, should I have been bearing with you.
text.KJV: 18:14 And when Paul was now about to open his mouth, Gallio said vnto the Iewes, If it were a matter of wrong, or wicked lewdnesse, O yee Iewes, reason would that I should beare with you.

text.NRSV: but since it is a matter of questions about words and names and your own law, see to it yourselves; I do not wish to be a judge of these matters.”
text.Rotherham: If, however, they are questions concerning discourse, and names, and law, that which ye have, ye shall see to it, yourselves; A judge of these things, I, am not disposed to be.
text.KJV: 18:15 But if it be a question of words, and names, and of your law, looke ye to it: for I wil be no iudge of such matters.

text.NRSV: And he dismissed them from the tribunal.
text.Rotherham: And he drove them from the judgment-seat.
text.KJV: 18:16 And he draue them from the iudgment seate.

text.NRSV: Then all of them seized Sosthenes, the official of the synagogue, and beat him in front of the tribunal. But Gallio paid no attention to any of these things.
text.Rotherham: But they all, laying hold of Sosthenes the ruler of the synagogue, began to strike him before the judgment-seat; and, for none of these things, did Gallio care.
text.KJV: 18:17 Then all the Greekes tooke Sosthenes the chiefe ruler of the Synagogue, and beat him before the Iudgement seat: and Gallio cared for none of those things.

text.NRSV: After staying there for a considerable time, Paul said farewell to the believers and sailed for Syria, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila. At Cenchreae he had his hair cut, for he was under a vow.
text.Rotherham: Paul, however, still further abiding a good many days with the brethren, bidding them adieu, set sail for Syria; and, with him, Priscilla and Aquila; having shorn his head in Cenchreae, for he had a vow.
text.KJV: 18:18 ¶ And Paul after this taried there yet a good while, and then tooke his leaue of the brethren, and sailed thence into Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila: hauing shorne his head in Cenchrea: for he had a vow.

text.NRSV: When they reached Ephesus, he left them there, but first he himself went into the synagogue and had a discussion with the Jews.
text.Rotherham: And they came down to Ephesus; and, as for them, he left them there,—but, himself entering into the synagogue, he reasoned with the Jews.
text.KJV: 18:19 And he came to Ephesus, and left them there: but he himselfe entred into the Synagogue, and reasoned with the Iewes.

text.NRSV: When they asked him to stay longer, he declined;
text.Rotherham: And, when they requested him for a longer time to abide, he consented not;
text.KJV: 18:20 When they desired him to tary longer time with them, hee consented not:

text.NRSV: but on taking leave of them, he said, “I will return to you, if God wills.” Then he set sail from Ephesus.
text.Rotherham: but, bidding them adieu, and saying—Again, will I return unto you, God willing, he sailed away from Ephesus;
text.KJV: 18:21 But bade them farewell, saying, I must by all meanes keepe this feast that commeth, in Hierusalem; but I will returne againe vnto you, if God will: and he sailed from Ephesus.

text.NRSV: When he had landed at Caesarea, he went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
text.Rotherham: and, putting in at Caesarea, going up and saluting the assembly, went down unto Antioch;
text.KJV: 18:22 And when he had landed at Cesarea, and gone vp, and saluted the Church, he went downe to Antioch.

text.NRSV: After spending some time there he departed and went from place to place through the region of Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
text.Rotherham: and, spending some time, he went forth, passing through, in order, the country of Galatia and Phrygia, confirming all the disciples.
text.KJV: 18:23 And after he had spent some time there, hee departed, and went ouer all the countrey of Galatia and Phrygia in order, strengthening all the disciples.

text.NRSV: Now there came to Ephesus a Jew named Apollos, a native of Alexandria. He was an eloquent man, well-versed in the scriptures.
text.Rotherham: But, a certain Jew, Apollos by name, an Alexandrian by birth, a learned man, came down to Ephesus, being, mighty, in the Scriptures.
text.KJV: 18:24 ¶ And a certaine Iew, named Apollos, borne at Alexandria, an eloquent man, and mightie in the Scriptures, came to Ephesus.

text.NRSV: He had been instructed in the Way of the Lord; and he spoke with burning enthusiasm and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus, though he knew only the baptism of John.
text.Rotherham: The same, had been orally taught the way of the Lord, and, being fervent in his spirit, began speaking and teaching accurately, the things concerning Jesus,—properly knowing, only the immersion of John.
text.KJV: 18:25 This man was instructed in the way of the Lord, and being feruent in the spirit, he spake and taught diligently the things of the Lord, knowing onely the baptisme of Iohn.

text.NRSV: He began to speak boldly in the synagogue; but when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside and explained the Way of God to him more accurately.
text.Rotherham: The same, also began speaking boldly in the synagogue; and Priscilla and Aquila, hearing him, took him unto them, and, more accurately, expounded unto him the way of God.
text.KJV: 18:26 And he began to speake boldly in the Synagogue: whom when Aquila and Priscilla had heard, they tooke him vnto them, and expounded vnto him the way of God more perfectly.

text.NRSV: And when he wished to cross over to Achaia, the believers encouraged him and wrote to the disciples to welcome him. On his arrival he greatly helped those who through grace had become believers,
text.Rotherham: And, he being minded to pass through into Achaia, the brethren urgently wrote unto the disciples to welcome him,—who, arriving, was very useful unto them who had believed, with his gift;
text.KJV: 18:27 And when hee was disposed to passe into Achaia, the brethren wrote, exhorting the disciples to receiue him: who, when he was come, helped them much which had beleeued throgh grace.

text.NRSV: for he powerfully refuted the Jews in public, showing by the scriptures that the Messiah is Jesus.
text.Rotherham: for, with great force, began he confuting the Jews, publicly, shewing by the Scriptures that Jesus was, the Christ.
text.KJV: 18:28 For hee mightily conuinced the Iewes, and that publikely, shewing by the scriptures, that Iesus was Christ.

text.NRSV: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul passed through the interior regions and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while Apollos was in Corinth, Paul, passing through the upper parts, came to Ephesus, and found certain disciples;
text.KJV: 19:1 And it came to passe, that while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul hauing passed thorow the vpper coasts, came to Ephesus, and finding certaine disciples,

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you became believers?” They replied, “No, we have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: and he said unto them—Holy Spirit, received ye, when ye believed? And they said unto him—Nay! not even whether there is Holy Spirit, did we hear.
text.KJV: 19:2 He said vnto them, Haue ye receiued the holy Ghost since yee beleeued? And they saide vnto him, Wee haue not so much as heard whether there be any holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Into what then were you baptized?” They answered, “Into John’s baptism.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Into what, then, were ye immersed? And, they, said—Unto John’s immersion.
text.KJV: 19:3 And he said vnto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they saide, Unto Iohns Baptisme.

text.NRSV: Paul said, “John baptized with the baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the one who was to come after him, that is, in Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Paul—John, immersed with an immersion of repentance, unto the people, saying, that, on him who was coming after him, they should believe,—that is, on Jesus.
text.KJV: 19:4 Then saide Paul, Iohn verely baptized with the baptisme of repentance, saying vnto the people, that they should beleeue on him which should come after him, that is, on Christ Iesus.

text.NRSV: On hearing this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, when they heard [this], they were immersed into the name of the Lord Jesus;
text.KJV: 19:5 When they heard this, they were baptized in the Name of the Lord Iesus.

text.NRSV: When Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they spoke in tongues and prophesied—
text.Rotherham: and, Paul laying hands upon them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they began speaking with tongues and prophesying.
text.KJV: 19:6 And when Paul had laide his hands vpon them, the holy Ghost came on them, and they spake with tongues, and prophecied.

text.NRSV: altogether there were about twelve of them.
text.Rotherham: And all the men were about twelve.
text.KJV: 19:7 And all þ men were about twelue.

text.NRSV: He entered the synagogue and for three months spoke out boldly, and argued persuasively about the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: And, entering into the synagogue, he was speaking boldly for three months, reasoning and persuading concerning the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: 19:8 And hee went into the Synagogue, and spake boldly for the space of three moneths, disputing and perswading the things concerning the Kingdome of God.

text.NRSV: When some stubbornly refused to believe and spoke evil of the Way before the congregation, he left them, taking the disciples with him, and argued daily in the lecture hall of Tyrannus.
text.Rotherham: But, when some were hardening themselves, and refusing to be persuaded, speaking evil of the Way before the throng, withdrawing from them, he separated the disciples; day by day, reasoning in the school of Tyrannus.
text.KJV: 19:9 But when diuers were hardened, and beleeued not, but spake euill of that way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, disputing daily in the schoole of one Tyrannus.

text.NRSV: This continued for two years, so that all the residents of Asia, both Jews and Greeks, heard the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, this, took place for two years, so that, all who dwelt in Asia, heard the word of the Lord, both Jews and Greeks.
text.KJV: 19:10 And this continued by the space of two yeeres, so that all they which dwelt in Asia, heard the word of the Lord Iesus, both Iewes and Greeks.

text.NRSV: God did extraordinary miracles through Paul,
text.Rotherham: Mighty works, also, not the ordinary, God was working through the hands of Paul;
text.KJV: 19:11 And God wrought speciall miracles by the hands of Paul:

text.NRSV: so that when the handkerchiefs or aprons that had touched his skin were brought to the sick, their diseases left them, and the evil spirits came out of them.
text.Rotherham: so that, even unto the sick, were being carried from his body, handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases were departing from them, and, the wicked spirits, were going out.
text.KJV: 19:12 So that from his body were brought vnto the sicke handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases departed from them, and the euill spirits went out of them.

text.NRSV: Then some itinerant Jewish exorcists tried to use the name of the Lord Jesus over those who had evil spirits, saying, “I adjure you by the Jesus whom Paul proclaims.”
text.Rotherham: But certain also of the wandering Jews, exorcists, took in hand to be naming, over them that had the wicked spirits, the name of the Lord Jesus, saying—I adjure you, by Jesus whom Paul proclaimeth!
text.KJV: 19:13 ¶ Then certaine of the vagabond Iewes, exorcistes, tooke vpon them to call ouer them which had euill spirits, the Name of the Lord Iesus, saying, We adiure you by Iesus whom Paul preacheth.

text.NRSV: Seven sons of a Jewish high priest named Sceva were doing this.
text.Rotherham: And there were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jew, a High-priest, who, this thing, were doing.
text.KJV: 19:14 And there were seuen sonnes of one Sceua a Iewe, and chiefe of the Priests, which did so.

text.NRSV: But the evil spirit answered them, “Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are you?”
text.Rotherham: But the wicked spirit. Answering, said unto them—Jesus, [indeed], I am getting to know, and, Paul, I well-know,—but who are, ye?
text.KJV: 19:15 And the euill spirit answered, and said, Iesus I knowe, and Paul I know, but who are ye?

text.NRSV: Then the man with the evil spirit leaped on them, mastered them all, and so overpowered them that they fled out of the house naked and wounded.
text.Rotherham: And the man in whom was the wicked spirit, springing upon them, mastering them both, prevailed against them, so that, naked and wounded, fled they out of that house.
text.KJV: 19:16 And the man in whom the euill spirit was, leapt on them, and ouercame them, and preuailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.

text.NRSV: When this became known to all residents of Ephesus, both Jews and Greeks, everyone was awestruck; and the name of the Lord Jesus was praised.
text.Rotherham: And, this, became known to all—both Jews and Greeks—who were dwelling in Ephesus; and fear fell upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was being magnified.
text.KJV: 19:17 And this was knowen to all the Iewes and Greekes also dwelling at Ephesus, and feare fell on them all, and the Name of the Lord Iesus was magnified.

text.NRSV: Also many of those who became believers confessed and disclosed their practices.
text.Rotherham: Many also of them who had believed, were coming, making open confession, and renouncing their practices.
text.KJV: 19:18 And many that beleeued came, and confessed, and shewed their deedes.

text.NRSV: A number of those who practiced magic collected their books and burned them publicly; when the value of these was calculated, it was found to come to fifty thousand silver coins.
text.Rotherham: And, a good many of them who had practised the curious arts, bringing together the books, were burning them before all; and they reckoned up the prices of them, and found them fifty thousand pieces of silver.
text.KJV: 19:19 Many also of them which vsed curious arts, brought their bookes together and burned them before all men: and they counted the price of them, and found it fifty thousand pieces of siluer.

text.NRSV: So the word of the Lord grew mightily and prevailed.
text.Rotherham: Thus, with might, the Lord’s word, was growing and prevailing.
text.KJV: 19:20 So mightily grew the word of God, and preuailed.

text.NRSV: Now after these things had been accomplished, Paul resolved in the Spirit to go through Macedonia and Achaia, and then to go on to Jerusalem. He said, “After I have gone there, I must also see Rome.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when these things were fulfilled, Paul purposed in his spirit, going through Macedonia and Achaia, to be journeying unto Jerusalem, saying—After I have been there, Rome also, must I see!
text.KJV: 19:21 ¶ After these things were ended, Paul purposed in the spirit, when hee had passed thorow Macedonia and Athaia, to go to Hierusalem, saying, After I haue bin there, I must also see Rome.

text.NRSV: So he sent two of his helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he himself stayed for some time in Asia.
text.Rotherham: And, sending off into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself, held on awhile in Asia.
text.KJV: 19:22 So hee sent into Macedonia two of them that ministred vnto him, Timotheus and Erastus, but he himselfe stayed in Asia for a season.

text.NRSV: About that time no little disturbance arose concerning the Way.
text.Rotherham: And there arose during that season no small disturbance concerning the Way.
text.KJV: 19:23 And the same time there arose no small stirre about that way.

text.NRSV: A man named Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the artisans.
text.Rotherham: For, one Demetrius by name, a silversmith, making [silver] shrines, of Diana, used to bring unto the craftsmen no little business;
text.KJV: 19:24 For a certaine man named Demetrius, a siluer smith, which made siluer shrines for Diana, brought no small gaine vnto the craftsmen:

text.NRSV: These he gathered together, with the workers of the same trade, and said, “Men, you know that we get our wealth from this business.
text.Rotherham: gathering whom together, and them who in such things wrought, he said—Men! ye well know that, by this business, we have our prosperity;
text.KJV: 19:25 Whom he called together, with the workemen of like occupation, and said, Sirs, ye know that by this craft we haue our wealth.

text.NRSV: You also see and hear that not only in Ephesus but in almost the whole of Asia this Paul has persuaded and drawn away a considerable number of people by saying that gods made with hands are not gods.
text.Rotherham: and ye perceive and hear that, not only in Ephesus, but well-nigh in all Asia, this Paul, hath persuaded and turned away a considerable multitude, saying that they are, no gods, which, with hands, are made.
text.KJV: 19:26 Moreouer, ye see & heare, that not alone at Ephesus, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul hath perswaded and turned away much people, saying, that they bee no gods, which are made with hands.

text.NRSV: And there is danger not only that this trade of ours may come into disrepute but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be scorned, and she will be deprived of her majesty that brought all Asia and the world to worship her.”
text.Rotherham: And, not only is there danger that this our heritage into ill-repute may come, but even that the temple, of the great Goddess Diana, for nothing, may be counted; also that, even on the point of being pulled down, may be Her Majesty, whom, all Asia and the habitable world, do worship.
text.KJV: 19:27 So that not only this our craft is in danger to be set at nought: but also that the Temple of the great goddesse Diana should be despised, and her magnificence should be destroyed, whom all Asia, and the world worshippeth.

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were enraged and shouted, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”
text.Rotherham: Now, hearing this, and becoming full of wrath, they began crying aloud, saying—Great, is Diana of the Ephesians!
text.KJV: 19:28 And when they heard these sayings, they were ful of wrath, & cried out, saying, Great is Diana of þ Ephesians.

text.NRSV: The city was filled with the confusion; and they rushed together to the theater, dragging with them Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians who were Paul’s companions in travel.
text.Rotherham: And the city was filled with the confusion; they rushed also with one accord into the theatre, carrying off with them Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians, fellow-travellers of Paul.
text.KJV: 19:29 And the whole citie was filled with confusion, and hauing caught Gaius and Aristarchus men of Macedonia Pauls companions in trauaile, they rushed with one accord into the Theatre.

text.NRSV: Paul wished to go into the crowd, but the disciples would not let him;
text.Rotherham: But, Paul, being minded to enter in among the populace, the disciples would not suffer him.
text.KJV: 19:30 And when Paul would haue entred in vnto the people, the disciples suffered him not.

text.NRSV: even some officials of the province of Asia, who were friends of his, sent him a message urging him not to venture into the theater.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, certain of the Asiarchs also, being his friends, sending unto him, were beseeching him not to adventure himself into the theatre.
text.KJV: 19:31 And certaine of the chiefe of Asia, which were his friends, sent vnto him, desiring him that he would not aduenture himselfe into the Theatre.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile, some were shouting one thing, some another; for the assembly was in confusion, and most of them did not know why they had come together.
text.Rotherham: Others, indeed, were crying out something else; for the assembly had become confused, and, the greater part, knew not for what cause they had come together.
text.KJV: 19:32 Some therefore cried one thing, and some another: for the assembly was confused, and the more part knew not wherefore they were come together.

text.NRSV: Some of the crowd gave instructions to Alexander, whom the Jews had pushed forward. And Alexander motioned with his hand and wanted to make a defense before the people.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, out of the multitude, they bare aloft one Alexander, the Jews thrusting him forward; Alexander, however, waving his hand, was wishing to make his defense unto the populace.
text.KJV: 19:33 And they drew Alexander out of the multitude, the Iewes putting him forward. And Alexander beckened with the hand, and would haue made his defence vnto the people.

text.NRSV: But when they recognized that he was a Jew, for about two hours all of them shouted in unison, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”
text.Rotherham: But, recognizing that he was a Jew, one voice arose from all for about two hours, as they cried aloud—Great, is Diana of the Ephesians!
text.KJV: 19:34 But when they knew that he was a Iewe, all with one voyce about the space of two houres cried out, Great is Diana of the Ephesians.

text.NRSV: But when the town clerk had quieted the crowd, he said, “Citizens of Ephesus, who is there that does not know that the city of the Ephesians is the temple keeper of the great Artemis and of the statue that fell from heaven?
text.Rotherham: Howbeit the town-clerk, having calmed the multitude, saith—Ephesians! why, who is there of mankind that doth not acknowledge the city of Ephesians to be temple-keeper of the Great Diana, and of the [image] that fell from Jupiter?
text.KJV: 19:35 And when the towne clarke had appeased the people, he said, Ye men of Ephesus, what man is there þ knoweth not how that the citie of the Ephesians is a worshipper of the great goddesse Diana, and of the image which fell downe from Iupiter?

text.NRSV: Since these things cannot be denied, you ought to be quiet and do nothing rash.
text.Rotherham: As these things, then, cannot be denied, it is needful that ye be calmed at once, and, nothing rash, be doing.
text.KJV: 19:36 Seeing then that these things cannot be spoken against, ye ought to be quiet, and to doe nothing rashly.

text.NRSV: You have brought these men here who are neither temple robbers nor blasphemers of our goddess.
text.Rotherham: For ye have brought these men, neither as temple-robbers, nor as defaming our goddess.
text.KJV: 19:37 For ye haue brought hither these men, which are neither robbers of Churches, nor yet blasphemers of your goddesse:

text.NRSV: If therefore Demetrius and the artisans with him have a complaint against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls; let them bring charges there against one another.
text.Rotherham: If then, Demetrius, and the craftsmen with him, have against anyone an accusation, courts, are being held, and there are, proconsuls: let them accuse one another!
text.KJV: 19:38 Wherefore if Demetrius, and the craftesmen which are with him, haue a matter against any man, the law is open, and there are deputies, let them implead one another.

text.NRSV: If there is anything further you want to know, it must be settled in the regular assembly.
text.Rotherham: But, if after, something further, ye are seeking, in the regular assembly shall it be settled.
text.KJV: 19:39 But if yee enquire any thing concerning other matters, it shalbe determined in a lawfull assembly.

text.NRSV: For we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since there is no cause that we can give to justify this commotion.”
text.Rotherham: For we are, even in danger of being accused of riot, concerning this day, no cause at all, existing, by reference to which we shall be able to give a reason for this concourse.
text.KJV: 19:40 For we are in danger to be called in question for this dayes vprore, there being no cause whereby we may giue an accompt of this concourse.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he dismissed the assembly.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said, he dismissed the assembly.
text.KJV: 19:41 And when hee had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

text.NRSV: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples; and after encouraging them and saying farewell, he left for Macedonia.
text.Rotherham: But, after the tumult had ceased, Paul, sending for the disciples and exhorting them, took leave, and went forth to be journeying unto Macedonia.
text.KJV: 20:1 And after the vprore was ceased, Paul called vnto him the disciples, and imbraced them, & departed, for to go into Macedonia.

text.NRSV: When he had gone through those regions and had given the believers much encouragement, he came to Greece,
text.Rotherham: Passing through those parts, however, and exhorting them with much discourse, he came into Greece;
text.KJV: 20:2 And when he had gone ouer those parts, and had giuen them much exhortation, he came into Greece,

text.NRSV: where he stayed for three months. He was about to set sail for Syria when a plot was made against him by the Jews, and so he decided to return through Macedonia.
text.Rotherham: and, spending three months, when a plot was laid against him by the Jews, as he was about to sail to Syria, he determined to turn back through Macedonia.
text.KJV: 20:3 And there abode three moneths: and when the Iewes layed waite for him, as hee was about to saile into Syria, hee purposed to returne thorow Macedonia.

text.NRSV: He was accompanied by Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Beroea, by Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, by Gaius from Derbe, and by Timothy, as well as by Tychicus and Trophimus from Asia.
text.Rotherham: Now there were accompanying him, Sopater, son of Pyrrhus a Beroean; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe and Timothy; and, of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text.KJV: 20:4 And there accompanied him into Asia, Sopater of Berea: and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus, and Secundus, and Gaius of Derbe, and Timotheus: and of Asia Tychicus and Trophimus.

text.NRSV: They went on ahead and were waiting for us in Troas;
text.Rotherham: And, these, came and were waiting for us at Troas.
text.KJV: 20:5 These going before, taried for vs at Troas:

text.NRSV: but we sailed from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and in five days we joined them in Troas, where we stayed for seven days.
text.Rotherham: And, we, sailed forth, after the days of unleavened bread, from Philippi, and came unto them in Troas in five days, where we tarried seven days.
text.KJV: 20:6 And wee sailed away from Philippi, after the dayes of vnleauened bread, and came vnto them to Troas in fiue dayes, where we abode seuen daies.

text.NRSV: On the first day of the week, when we met to break bread, Paul was holding a discussion with them; since he intended to leave the next day, he continued speaking until midnight.
text.Rotherham: And, on the first of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread, Paul went on to discourse with them, being about to depart on the morrow; and he prolonged his discourse until midnight.
text.KJV: 20:7 And vpon the first day of the weeke, when the disciples came together to breake bread, Paul preached vnto them, ready to depart on the morrow, and continued his speach vntill midnight.

text.NRSV: There were many lamps in the room upstairs where we were meeting.
text.Rotherham: Now there were a good many torches in the upper room, where we were gathered together.
text.KJV: 20:8 And there were many lights in the vpper chamber where they were gathered together.

text.NRSV: A young man named Eutychus, who was sitting in the window, began to sink off into a deep sleep while Paul talked still longer. Overcome by sleep, he fell to the ground from the third story and was picked up dead.
text.Rotherham: And there sat, a certain young man by name Eutychus, in the window, who was getting overpowered by a deep sleep; and, while Paul was discoursing yet further, being overpowered by his sleep, he fell, from the third story, down, and was taken up dead.
text.KJV: 20:9 And there sate in a window a certaine yong man named Eutychus, being fallen into a deepe sleepe, and as Paul was long preaching, hee sunke downe with sleepe, and fel downe from the third loft, and was taken vp dead.

text.NRSV: But Paul went down, and bending over him took him in his arms, and said, “Do not be alarmed, for his life is in him.”
text.Rotherham: Going down, however, Paul fell upon him, and, embracing him, said—Be not making confusion; for, his soul, is, in him.
text.KJV: 20:10 And Paul went downe, and fell on him, and embracing him, saide, Trouble not your selues, for his life is in him.

text.NRSV: Then Paul went upstairs, and after he had broken bread and eaten, he continued to converse with them until dawn; then he left.
text.Rotherham: And, going up, and breaking the loaf, and tasting,—for a good while also conversing, until dawn, thus, he departed.
text.KJV: 20:11 When hee therefore was come vp againe, & had broken bread, and eaten, and talked a long while, euen till breake of day, so he departed.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile they had taken the boy away alive and were not a little comforted.
text.Rotherham: And they brought the boy alive, and were comforted beyond measure.
text.KJV: 20:12 And they brought the yong man aliue, and were not a little comforted.

text.NRSV: We went on ahead to the ship and set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul on board there; for he had made this arrangement, intending to go by land himself.
text.Rotherham: And, we, going forward unto the ship, set sail for Assos, from thence being about to take up Paul; for, so, had he arranged, being about, himself, to go on foot.
text.KJV: 20:13 ¶ And wee went before to ship, and sailed vnto Assos, there intending to take in Paul: for so had hee appointed, minding himselfe to goe afoote.

text.NRSV: When he met us in Assos, we took him on board and went to Mitylene.
text.Rotherham: And, when he fell in with us in Assos, we took him on board, and came into Mitylene;
text.KJV: 20:14 And when he met with vs at Assos, wee tooke him in, and came to Mitylene.

text.NRSV: We sailed from there, and on the following day we arrived opposite Chios. The next day we touched at Samos, and the day after that we came to Miletus.
text.Rotherham: and, from thence sailing away on the morrow, we came over against Chios, and, on the next day, we thrust aside into Samos, and, on the succeeding day, we came into Miletus.
text.KJV: 20:15 And wee sailed thence, and came the next day ouer against Chios, and the next day we arriued at Samos, and taried at Trogyllium: and the next day we came to Miletus.

text.NRSV: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus, so that he might not have to spend time in Asia; he was eager to be in Jerusalem, if possible, on the day of Pentecost.
text.Rotherham: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus, lest he should happen to lose time in Asia; for he hastened, if it were possible for him, against the day of Pentecost, to arrive, in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 20:16 For Paul had determined to saile by Ephesus, because he would not spend the time in Asia: for he hasted, if it were possible for him, to be at Hierusalem the day of Pentecost.

text.NRSV: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus, asking the elders of the church to meet him.
text.Rotherham: But, from Miletus, he sent unto Ephesus, and called for the elders of the assembly.
text.KJV: 20:17 ¶ And from Miletus hee sent to Ephesus, and called the Elders of the Church.

text.NRSV: When they came to him, he said to them: “You yourselves know how I lived among you the entire time from the first day that I set foot in Asia,
text.Rotherham: And, when they were come to him, he said unto them—Ye yourselves, well know, from the first day when I set foot in Asia, in what manner came to be with you all the time,
text.KJV: 20:18 And when they were come to him, he said vnto them, Ye know from the first day that I came into Asia, after what maner I haue bene with you at all seasons,

text.NRSV: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears, enduring the trials that came to me through the plots of the Jews;
text.Rotherham: doing service unto the Lord, with all humility and tears, and temptations which befell me through the plots of the Jews:
text.KJV: 20:19 Seruing the Lord with all humilitie of minde, and with many teares, and temptations, which befell me by the lying in wait of the Iewes:

text.NRSV: I did not shrink from doing anything helpful, proclaiming the message to you and teaching you publicly and from house to house,
text.Rotherham: in what manner I in nowise shrank from announcing unto you anything that was profitable and teaching you publicly and in your homes;
text.KJV: 20:20 And how I kept backe nothing that was profitable vnto you, but haue shewed you, and haue taught you publikely, and from house to house,

text.NRSV: as I testified to both Jews and Greeks about repentance toward God and faith toward our Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: bearing full witness, both to Jews and to Greeks, as to the repentance due unto God, and as to belief on our Lord Jesus.
text.KJV: 20:21 Testifying both to the Iewes and also to the Greekes, repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Iesus Christ.

text.NRSV: And now, as a captive to the Spirit, I am on my way to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there,
text.Rotherham: And, now, lo! I, bound in my spirit, am journeying unto Jerusalem; the things which therein shall befall me, not knowing,—
text.KJV: 20:22 And now behold, I goe bound in the spirit vnto Hierusalem, not knowing the things that shal befall me there:

text.NRSV: except that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city that imprisonment and persecutions are waiting for me.
text.Rotherham: save that, the Holy Spirit, from city to city, doth bear me full witness, saying that, bonds and tribulations, await me.
text.KJV: 20:23 Saue that the holy Ghost witnesseth in euery city, saying that bonds and afflictions abide me.

text.NRSV: But I do not count my life of any value to myself, if only I may finish my course and the ministry that I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the good news of God’s grace.
text.Rotherham: But, for no cause whatever, am I making my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my course and the ministry which I have received from the Lord Jesus, to bear full witness as to the good news of the favour of God.
text.KJV: 20:24 But none of these things mooue me, neither count I my life deare vnto my self, so that I might finish my course with ioy, & the ministery which I haue receiued of the Lord Iesus, to testifie the Gospel of the grace of God.

text.NRSV: “And now I know that none of you, among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom, will ever see my face again.
text.Rotherham: And, now, lo! I, know that, no more, shall ye see my face,—ye all, among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom.
text.KJV: 20:25 And now behold, I know that ye all, among whom I haue gone preaching the kingdom of God, shall see my face no more.

text.NRSV: Therefore I declare to you this day that I am not responsible for the blood of any of you,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore I take you to witness, on this very day, that, pure am I from the blood of all;
text.KJV: 20:26 Wherefore I take you to record this day, that I am pure from the blood of all men.

text.NRSV: for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole purpose of God.
text.Rotherham: for I have not shrunk from announcing all the counsel of God unto you.
text.KJV: 20:27 For I haue not shunned to declare vnto you all the counsell of God.

text.NRSV: Keep watch over yourselves and over all the flock, of which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the church of God that he obtained with the blood of his own Son.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed unto yourselves, and unto all the little flock in which the Holy Spirit hath set, you, as overseers,—to be shepherding the assembly of God which he hath acquired through means of the blood of his own.
text.KJV: 20:28 ¶ Take heed therefore vnto your selues, & to all the flocke, ouer the which the holy Ghost hath made you ouerseers, to feed the Church of God, which he hath purchased with his own blood.

text.NRSV: I know that after I have gone, savage wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock.
text.Rotherham: I, know, that there will enter, after my departure, grievous wolves into your midst, not sparing the little flock;
text.KJV: 20:29 For I know this, that after my departing shall grieuous wolues enter in among you, not sparing the flocke.

text.NRSV: Some even from your own group will come distorting the truth in order to entice the disciples to follow them.
text.Rotherham: and, from among your own selves, will arise men speaking distorted things, to draw away the disciples after themselves.
text.KJV: 20:30 Also of your owne selues shal men arise, speaking peruerse things, to draw away disciples after them.

text.NRSV: Therefore be alert, remembering that for three years I did not cease night or day to warn everyone with tears.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, be on the watch, remembering that, for three years, night and day, I gave myself no rest, with tears, admonishing each one.
text.KJV: 20:31 Therefore watch, and remember that by the space of three yeeres, I ceased not to warne euery one night and day with teares.

text.NRSV: And now I commend you to God and to the message of his grace, a message that is able to build you up and to give you the inheritance among all who are sanctified.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I commend you unto the Lord, and unto his word of favour,—which is able to build up and give the inheritance among all the hallowed ones.
text.KJV: 20:32 And now brethren, I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you vp, and to giue you an inheritance among all them which are sanctified.

text.NRSV: I coveted no one’s silver or gold or clothing.
text.Rotherham: The silver or gold or apparel of no one, did I covet:
text.KJV: 20:33 I haue coueted no mans siluer, or golde, or apparell.

text.NRSV: You know for yourselves that I worked with my own hands to support myself and my companions.
text.Rotherham: yourselves, acknowledge that, for my necessities and for those who were with me, hard wrought these hands!
text.KJV: 20:34 Yea, you your selues know, that these handes haue ministred vnto my necessities, and to them that were with me.

text.NRSV: In all this I have given you an example that by such work we must help the weak, remembering the words of the Lord Jesus, for he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’ ”
text.Rotherham: In all things, I gave you to understand, that, thus toiling, it behoves to be helping the weak, also to be keeping in mind the words of the Lord Jesus, that, he himself, said—Happy is it, rather to give than to receive!
text.KJV: 20:35 I haue shewed you all things, how that so labouring, yee ought to support the weake, and to remember the words of the Lord Iesus, how he said, It is more blessed to giue, then to receiue.

text.NRSV: When he had finished speaking, he knelt down with them all and prayed.
text.Rotherham: And, these things saying, kneeling down with them all, he prayed.
text.KJV: 20:36 ¶ And when he had thus spoken, he kneeled downe, & prayed with them all.

text.NRSV: There was much weeping among them all; they embraced Paul and kissed him,
text.Rotherham: And they all wept much, and, falling upon Paul’s neck, they were tenderly kissing him;
text.KJV: 20:37 And they all wept sore, and fell on Pauls necke, and kissed him,

text.NRSV: grieving especially because of what he had said, that they would not see his face again. Then they brought him to the ship.
text.Rotherham: being most distressed for the word which he had said,—That, no more, should they, his face, behold. And they accompanied him unto the ship.
text.KJV: 20:38 Sorrowing most of all for the words which he spake, that they should see his face no more. And they accompanied him vnto the ship.

text.NRSV: When we had parted from them and set sail, we came by a straight course to Cos, and the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when we set sail, having torn ourselves from them, running a straight course, we came unto Cos, and, on the next day, unto Rhodes,—and from thence unto Patara;
text.KJV: 21:1 And it came to passe, that after wee were gotten frō them, and had lanched, wee came with a straight course vnto Choos, and the day following vnto Rhodes, and from thence vnto Patara.

text.NRSV: When we found a ship bound for Phoenicia, we went on board and set sail.
text.Rotherham: and, finding a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, going on board, we set sail.
text.KJV: 21:2 And finding a ship sailing ouer vnto Phenicea, wee went abroad, and set foorth.

text.NRSV: We came in sight of Cyprus; and leaving it on our left, we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre, because the ship was to unload its cargo there.
text.Rotherham: And, sighting Cyprus, and leaving it behind to the left, we held on our voyage to Syria, and landed at Tyre; for, there, the ship was to discharge her cargo.
text.KJV: 21:3 Now when wee had discouered Cyprus, we left it on the left hand, and sailed into Syria, and landed at Tyre: for there the shippe was to vnlade her burden.

text.NRSV: We looked up the disciples and stayed there for seven days. Through the Spirit they told Paul not to go on to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, finding up the disciples, we remained there seven days, and they, unto Paul, began to say, through the Spirit, that he would gain no footing in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 21:4 And finding disciples, wee taried there seuen dayes: who said to Paul through the Spirit, that hee should not goe vp to Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: When our days there were ended, we left and proceeded on our journey; and all of them, with wives and children, accompanied us outside the city. There we knelt down on the beach and prayed
text.Rotherham: And, when it came to pass that we had completed the days, we went forth, and continued our journey, all of them accompanying us, with wives and children, as far as outside the city; and, kneeling down on the beach in prayer,
text.KJV: 21:5 And when we had accomplished those dayes, we departed, and went our way, and they all brought vs on our way, with wiues and children, till wee were out of the citie: and wee kneeled downe on the shore, and prayed.

text.NRSV: and said farewell to one another. Then we went on board the ship, and they returned home.
text.Rotherham: we tare ourselves from each other, and we went on board the ship, while, they, returned unto their homes.
text.KJV: 21:6 And when we had taken our leaue one of another, we tooke ship, and they returned home againe.

text.NRSV: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre, we arrived at Ptolemais; and we greeted the believers and stayed with them for one day.
text.Rotherham: And, we, finishing the voyage from Tyre, reached Ptolemais, and, saluting the brethren, abode one day with them;
text.KJV: 21:7 And when wee had finished our course from Tyre, wee came to Ptolemais, and saluted the brethren, and abode with them one day.

text.NRSV: The next day we left and came to Caesarea; and we went into the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the seven, and stayed with him.
text.Rotherham: and, on the morrow departing, we came to Caesarea, and, entering into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was of the seven, we abode with him.
text.KJV: 21:8 And the next day we that were of Pauls company, departed, and came vnto Cesarea, and wee entred into the house of Philip the Euangelist (which was one of the seuen) & abode with him.

text.NRSV: He had four unmarried daughters who had the gift of prophecy.
text.Rotherham: Now—this man, had four virgin daughters, who used to prophesy.
text.KJV: 21:9 And þ same man had foure daughters, virgins, which did prophesie.

text.NRSV: While we were staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
text.Rotherham: And, as we stayed on many days, there came down a certain man from Judaea, a prophet, by name Agabus;
text.KJV: 21:10 And as wee taried there many dayes, there came downe from Iudea a certaine Prophet, named Agabus.

text.NRSV: He came to us and took Paul’s belt, bound his own feet and hands with it, and said, “Thus says the Holy Spirit, ‘This is the way the Jews in Jerusalem will bind the man who owns this belt and will hand him over to the Gentiles.’ ”
text.Rotherham: and, coming unto us, and taking Paul’s girdle, he bound his own feet and hands, and said—Thus, saith the Holy Spirit, the man whose this girdle is, shall the Jews thus bind in Jerusalem, and deliver up into the hands of Gentiles.
text.KJV: 21:11 And when he was come vnto vs, he tooke Pauls girdle, and bound his owne hands and feete, and said, Thus sayth the holy Ghost, So shall the Iewes at Hierusalem binde the man that oweth this girdle, and shall deliuer him into the hands of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: When we heard this, we and the people there urged him not to go up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, when we heard these things, both, we, and they of the place began beseeching him, not to go up unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 21:12 And when we heard these things, both we and they of that place, besought him not to goe vp to Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: Then Paul answered, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound but even to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: Then, answered Paul—What are ye doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For, I, not only to be bound, but to die in Jerusalem, am ready, in behalf of the name of the Lord Jesus.
text.KJV: 21:13 Then Paul answered, What meane ye to weepe and to breake mine heart? for I am ready, not to bee bound onely, but also to die at Hierusalem for the Name of the Lord Iesus.

text.NRSV: Since he would not be persuaded, we remained silent except to say, “The Lord’s will be done.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was not to be persuaded, we ceased, saying—the Lord’s, will be done!
text.KJV: 21:14 And when he would not bee perswaded, we ceased, saying, The will of the Lord be done.

text.NRSV: After these days we got ready and went up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, after these days, making ready what we had, we started to go up unto Jerusalem;
text.KJV: 21:15 And after those dayes we tooke vp our cariages, & went vp to Hierusalem.

text.NRSV: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also came along and brought us to the house of Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we were to stay.
text.Rotherham: and there went certain also of the disciples from Caesarea, along with us, who were to introduce us unto one with whom we might be entertained, one Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple.
text.KJV: 21:16 There went with vs also certaine of the disciples of Cesarea, and brought with them one Mnason of Cyprus, an old disciple, with whō we should lodge.

text.NRSV: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers welcomed us warmly.
text.Rotherham: Now, when we came to Jerusalem, the brethren, gladly welcomed us.
text.KJV: 21:17 And when we were come to Hierusalem, the brethren receiued vs gladly

text.NRSV: The next day Paul went with us to visit James; and all the elders were present.
text.Rotherham: And, on the next day, Paul went in with us unto James, and, all the elders, were present.
text.KJV: 21:18 And the day following Paul went in with vs vnto Iames, and all the Elders were present.

text.NRSV: After greeting them, he related one by one the things that God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
text.Rotherham: And, saluting them, he went on to narrate, one by one, each of the things which God had wrought among the nations through his ministry.
text.KJV: 21:19 And when hee had saluted them, hee declared particularly what things God had wrought among the Gentiles by his ministerie.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they praised God. Then they said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands of believers there are among the Jews, and they are all zealous for the law.
text.Rotherham: And, they, having heard, began glorifying God; and they said to him—Thou observest, brother, how many myriads there are among the Jews who have believed, and, all, are zealous for the law.
text.KJV: 21:20 And when they heard it, they glorified the Lord, & said vnto him, Thou seest, brother, how many thousands of Iewes there are which beleeue, and they are all zealous of the Law.

text.NRSV: They have been told about you that you teach all the Jews living among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, and that you tell them not to circumcise their children or observe the customs.
text.Rotherham: Now they have heard it rumoured concerning thee, that, an apostacy, art thou teaching, from Moses, unto all the Jews, who are among the nations, telling them not to be circumcising their children, nor, by the customs, to be walking.
text.KJV: 21:21 And they are informed of thee, that thou teachest all the Iewes which are among the Gentiles, to forsake Moses, saying, that they ought not to circumcise their children, neither to walke after the customes.

text.NRSV: What then is to be done? They will certainly hear that you have come.
text.Rotherham: What, then, is it? at all events, they will hear that thou hast come.
text.KJV: 21:22 What is it therefore? the multitude must needs come together: for they will heare that thou art come.

text.NRSV: So do what we tell you. We have four men who are under a vow.
text.Rotherham: This, then, do, which unto thee, we say:—We have four men who have a vow upon themselves.
text.KJV: 21:23 Doe therefore this that we say to thee: Wee haue foure men which haue a vow on them,

text.NRSV: Join these men, go through the rite of purification with them, and pay for the shaving of their heads. Thus all will know that there is nothing in what they have been told about you, but that you yourself observe and guard the law.
text.Rotherham: Taking these unto thee, be purified with them, and spend something upon them, that they may shave their head; and all will get to know, that the things which they have heard rumoured concerning thee, are, nothing,—on the contrary, thou thyself, dost keep the ranks, guarding the law.
text.KJV: 21:24 Then take, and purifie thy selfe with them, & bee at charges with them, that they may shaue their heads: and all may know that those things wherof they were informed concerning thee, are nothing, but that thou thy selfe also walkest orderly, and keepest the Law.

text.NRSV: But as for the Gentiles who have become believers, we have sent a letter with our judgment that they should abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what is strangled and from fornication.”
text.Rotherham: But, concerning them of the nations who have believed, we ourselves, sent, deciding that they should be guarding themselves, both as to idol sacrifice, and blood, and what is strangled, and fornication.
text.KJV: 21:25 As touching the Gentiles which beleeue, wee haue written and concluded, that they obserue no such thing, saue onely that they keepe themselues from things offered to idoles, and from blood, and from strangled, and from fornication.

text.NRSV: Then Paul took the men, and the next day, having purified himself, he entered the temple with them, making public the completion of the days of purification when the sacrifice would be made for each of them.
text.Rotherham: Then Paul, taking unto him the men, on the next day, with them, being purified, began entering into the temple to declare the filling up of the days of the purification—until, the offering, had been presented for each one of them.
text.KJV: 21:26 Then Paul tooke the men, and the next day purifying himselfe with them, entred into the Temple, to signifie the accomplishment of the dayes of purification, vntill that an offering should be offered for euery one of them:

text.NRSV: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, who had seen him in the temple, stirred up the whole crowd. They seized him,
text.Rotherham: When, however, the seven days were on the point of being concluded, the Jews from Asia, observing him in the temple, began to urge-on all the multitude, and thrust upon them their hands,
text.KJV: 21:27 And when the seuen dayes were almost ended, the Iewes which were of Asia, when they saw him in the Temple, stirred vp all the people, and laide hands on him,

text.NRSV: shouting, “Fellow Israelites, help! This is the man who is teaching everyone everywhere against our people, our law, and this place; more than that, he has actually brought Greeks into the temple and has defiled this holy place.”
text.Rotherham: crying out—Israelites! be giving help! This, is the man who, against the people, and the law, and this place, is teaching, all men everywhere; furthermore, even Greeks, hath he brought into the temple, and hath profaned this holy place.
text.KJV: 21:28 Crying out, Men of Israel, helpe: this is þ man that teacheth al men euery where against the people, and the law, and this place: and farther brought Greeks also into the Temple, and hath polluted this holy place.

text.NRSV: (For they had previously seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.)
text.Rotherham: For they had before seen Trophimus, the Ephesian, in the city along with him, whom they were supposing, Paul, had brought, into the temple.
text.KJV: 21:29 (For they had seene before with him in the citie, Trophimus an Ephesian, whome they supposed that Paul had brought into the Temple.)

text.NRSV: Then all the city was aroused, and the people rushed together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple, and immediately the doors were shut.
text.Rotherham: And the whole city was set in motion, and there took place a running together of the people, and, laying hold of Paul, they proceeded to drag him outside the temple, and, straightway, the doors were made fast.
text.KJV: 21:30 And all the citie was moued, and the people ran together: and they tooke Paul, and drew him out of the Temple: and forthwith the doores were shut.

text.NRSV: While they were trying to kill him, word came to the tribune of the cohort that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.
text.Rotherham: When, also, they were seeking to slay him, there was carried up information, unto the captain of the band, that all Jerusalem was in confusion:—
text.KJV: 21:31 And as they went about to kil him, tidings came vnto the chiefe captaine of the band, that all Hierusalem was in an vprore.

text.NRSV: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them. When they saw the tribune and the soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text.Rotherham: who, instantly taking unto him soldiers and centurions, ran down upon them; and, they, seeing the captain and the soldiers, left off striking Paul.
text.KJV: 21:32 Who immediatly tooke souldiers, and Centurions, and ran downe vnto them: and when they saw the chiefe captaine and the souldiers, they left beating of Paul.

text.NRSV: Then the tribune came, arrested him, and ordered him to be bound with two chains; he inquired who he was and what he had done.
text.Rotherham: Then, the captain, drawing near, laid hold of him, and ordered him to be bound with two chains,—and began to enquire, who he might be, and what he had done;
text.KJV: 21:33 Then the chiefe captain came neere, and tooke him, & commanded him to be bound with two chains, and demanded who he was, and what hee had done.

text.NRSV: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, some another; and as he could not learn the facts because of the uproar, he ordered him to be brought into the barracks.
text.Rotherham: but, others, were calling out, something else, in the multitude; and so, as he could not get to know the certainty, because of the tumult, he ordered him to be brought into the castle.
text.KJV: 21:34 And some cried one thing, some another, among the multitude: and when he could not know the certaintie for the tumult, he commanded him to be caried into the castle.

text.NRSV: When Paul came to the steps, the violence of the mob was so great that he had to be carried by the soldiers.
text.Rotherham: And, when he came unto the stairs, so it was that he was borne along by the soldiers, because of the force of the multitude;
text.KJV: 21:35 And when he came vpon þ staires, so it was that he was borne of the souldiers, for the violence of the people.

text.NRSV: The crowd that followed kept shouting, “Away with him!”
text.Rotherham: for the throng of the people was following, crying out—Away with him!
text.KJV: 21:36 For the multitude of the people followed after, crying, Away with him.

text.NRSV: Just as Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he said to the tribune, “May I say something to you?” The tribune replied, “Do you know Greek?
text.Rotherham: But, when he was on the point of being taken into the castle, Paul saith unto the captain—Is it allowed me, to say somewhat unto thee? And he said—With Greek, art thou acquainted?
text.KJV: 21:37 And as Paul was to bee led into the castle, hee saide vnto the chiefe captaine, May I speake vnto thee? Who saide, Canst thou speake Greeke?

text.NRSV: Then you are not the Egyptian who recently stirred up a revolt and led the four thousand assassins out into the wilderness?”
text.Rotherham: Not, then, art, thou, the Egyptian, who, before these days, stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four-thousand men of the Assassins?
text.KJV: 21:38 Art not thou that Egyptian which before these daies madest an vprore, and leddest out into the wildernesse foure thousand men that were murtherers?

text.NRSV: Paul replied, “I am a Jew, from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city; I beg you, let me speak to the people.”
text.Rotherham: And Paul said—I, indeed, am a Jew, of Tarsus in Cilicia,—a citizen, of no obscure city; but I beseech thee, give me leave to speak unto the people!
text.KJV: 21:39 But Paul said, I am a man which am a Iew of Tarsus, a citie in Cilicia, a citizen of no meane citie: & I beseech thee suffer me to speake vnto the people.

text.NRSV: When he had given him permission, Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people with his hand; and when there was a great hush, he addressed them in the Hebrew language.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had given leave, Paul, standing upon the stairs, waved with his hand unto the people; and, when great silence was secured, he addressed them in the Hebrew language, saying—
text.KJV: 21:40 And when he had giuen him licence, Paul stood on the staires, and beckened with the hand vnto the people: and when there was made a great silence, he spake vnto them in the Hebrew tongue, saying.

text.NRSV: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense that I now make before you.”
text.Rotherham: Brethren and fathers! Hear ye, the defence, which I now make unto you:—
text.KJV: 22:1 Men, brethren, and fathers, heare ye my defence which I make now vnto you.

text.NRSV: When they heard him addressing them in Hebrew, they became even more quiet. Then he said:
text.Rotherham: And, when they heard that, in the Hebrew language, he had begun to address them, they kept the more quiet. And he saith—
text.KJV: 22:2 (And when they heard that hee spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)

text.NRSV: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, educated strictly according to our ancestral law, being zealous for God, just as all of you are today.
text.Rotherham: I, am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but nurtured in this city, at the feet of Gamaliel,—trained after the strictness of our ancestral law; being jealous for God just as, all ye, are this day;
text.KJV: 22:3 I am verely a man which am a Iew, borne in Tarsus a citie in Cilicia, yet brought vp in this citie at the feete of Gamaliel, and taught according to the perfect maner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous towards God, as ye all are this day.

text.NRSV: I persecuted this Way up to the point of death by binding both men and women and putting them in prison,
text.Rotherham: and, this way, I persecuted unto the death, binding and delivering up into prisons both men and women:—
text.KJV: 22:4 And I persecuted this way vnto the death, binding and deliuering into prisons both men and women,

text.NRSV: as the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me. From them I also received letters to the brothers in Damascus, and I went there in order to bind those who were there and to bring them back to Jerusalem for punishment.
text.Rotherham: as, even the High-priest, beareth me witness, and all the Eldership,—from whom, letters also, accepting to the brethren, unto Damascus, was I journeying, to bring them who were there bound unto Jerusalem, that they might be punished.
text.KJV: 22:5 As also the high Priest doth beare me witnesse, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I receiued letters vnto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there, bound vnto Hierusalem, for to be punished.

text.NRSV: “While I was on my way and approaching Damascus, about noon a great light from heaven suddenly shone around me.
text.Rotherham: But it befell me, as I was journeying and drawing nigh unto Damascus, that, about mid-day—suddenly—out of heaven, there flashed a great light all around me;
text.KJV: 22:6 And it came to passe, that as I made my iourney, & was come nigh vnto Damascus about noone, suddenly there shone from heauen a great light round about me.

text.NRSV: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
text.Rotherham: I fell also to the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me—Saul! Saul! Why, me, art thou persecuting?
text.KJV: 22:7 And I fell vnto the ground, and heard a voice saying vnto mee, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

text.NRSV: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ Then he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth whom you are persecuting.’
text.Rotherham: And, I, answered—Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me—I, am Jesus the Nazarene, whom, thou, art persecuting!
text.KJV: 22:8 And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said vnto me, I am Iesus of Nazareth whō thou persecutest.

text.NRSV: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not hear the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
text.Rotherham: Now, they who were with me, beheld, indeed, the light, but heard not, the voice, of him that was speaking with me
text.KJV: 22:9 And they that were with me saw indeede the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.

text.NRSV: I said, ‘What am I to do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Get up and go to Damascus; there you will be told everything that has been assigned to you to do.’
text.Rotherham: And said—What shall I do, Lord? And, the Lord, said unto me—Arise, and be going thy way into Damascus, and, there, shall it be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
text.KJV: 22:10 And I saide, What shall I doe, Lord? And the Lord said vnto me, Arise, and goe into Damascus, and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to doe.

text.NRSV: Since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, those who were with me took my hand and led me to Damascus.
text.Rotherham: But, as I could not see clearly owing to the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them who were with me, I came into Damascus.
text.KJV: 22:11 And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.

text.NRSV: “A certain Ananias, who was a devout man according to the law and well spoken of by all the Jews living there,
text.Rotherham: And, one Ananias, a man devout according to the law, well-attested by all the Jews that dwelt there,
text.KJV: 22:12 And one Ananias, a deuout man according to the law, hauing a good report of al the Iewes which dwelt there,

text.NRSV: came to me; and standing beside me, he said, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight!’ In that very hour I regained my sight and saw him.
text.Rotherham: coming unto me, and standing over me, said—Saul, brother! look up. And, I, in that very hour, looked up on him.
text.KJV: 22:13 Came vnto me, and stood, & said vnto me, Brother Saul, receiue thy sight. And the same houre I looked vp vpon him.

text.NRSV: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear his own voice;
text.Rotherham: And he said—The God of our fathers, hath chosen thee to get to know his will, and to see the Righteous One,—and to hear a voice out of his mouth.
text.KJV: 22:14 And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, þ thou shouldest know his will, & see that Iust one, and shouldest heare the voice of his mouth.

text.NRSV: for you will be his witness to all the world of what you have seen and heard.
text.Rotherham: Because thou shalt be a witness to him unto all men, of the things which thou hast seen and heard.
text.KJV: 22:15 For thou shalt be his witnes vnto al men, of what thou hast seene & heard.

text.NRSV: And now why do you delay? Get up, be baptized, and have your sins washed away, calling on his name.’
text.Rotherham: And, now, what art thou going to do? Arise, and get thyself immersed, and have thy sins bathed away, calling upon his name.
text.KJV: 22:16 And now, why tariest thou? Arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sinnes, calling on the name of the Lord.

text.NRSV: “After I had returned to Jerusalem and while I was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when had returned unto Jerusalem, and was praying in the temple, that I came to be in a trance,
text.KJV: 22:17 And it came to passe, that when I was come againe to Hierusalem, euen while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance,

text.NRSV: and saw Jesus saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
text.Rotherham: and saw him, saying unto me—Haste thee, and go forth speedily out of Jerusalem, inasmuch as they will not accept thy witness concerning me.
text.KJV: 22:18 And saw him saying vnto mee, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Hierusalem: for they will not receiue thy testimony concerning me.

text.NRSV: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
text.Rotherham: And, I, said—Lord! they themselves, well know that I was imprisoning and beating in every synagogue them who were believing on thee.
text.KJV: 22:19 And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned, and beat in euery synagogue them that beleeued on thee.

text.NRSV: And while the blood of your witness Stephen was shed, I myself was standing by, approving and keeping the coats of those who were killing him.’
text.Rotherham: And, when the blood of Stephen thy witness was being shed, even I myself, was standing by, and approving, and guarding the mantles of them who were slaying him.
text.KJV: 22:20 And when þ blood of thy martyr Steuen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting vnto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’ ”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me—Be taking thy journey; because, I, unto nations afar off, will send thee.
text.KJV: 22:21 And he said vnto me, Depart: for I will send thee farre hence, vnto the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: Up to this point they listened to him, but then they shouted, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
text.Rotherham: And they hearkened unto him as far as this word, and lifted up their voice, saying—Away from the earth, with such a man as this, for it is not fit that he should live.
text.KJV: 22:22 And they gaue him audience vnto this word, and then lift vp their voices, and said, Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should liue.

text.NRSV: While they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and tossing dust into the air,
text.Rotherham: Now, as they were both making an outcry and tearing their mantles,—dust, also were throwing into the air,
text.KJV: 22:23 And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, & threw dust into the aire,

text.NRSV: the tribune directed that he was to be brought into the barracks, and ordered him to be examined by flogging, to find out why they were shouting against him in this way.
text.Rotherham: the captain ordered him to be brought into the castle, saying, that, with scourging, he should be put to the test,—that he might find out, for what cause they were, thus, clamouring against him.
text.KJV: 22:24 The chiefe captaine commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that hee should be examined by scourging: that he might know wherfore they cried so against him.

text.NRSV: But when they had tied him up with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it legal for you to flog a Roman citizen who is uncondemned?”
text.Rotherham: But, when they had stretched him out with straps, Paul said unto the by-standing centurion—A Roman, and uncondemned, is it allowed you to be scourging?
text.KJV: 22:25 And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said vnto the Centurion that stood by, Is it lawfull for you to scourge a man that is a Romane, and vncondemned?

text.NRSV: When the centurion heard that, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.”
text.Rotherham: And, when the centurion heard [that], he went unto the captain, and reported, saying—What art thou going to do? For, this man, is, a Roman?
text.KJV: 22:26 When the Centurion heard that, hee went and told the chiefe captaine, saying, Take heede what thou doest, for this man is a Romane.

text.NRSV: The tribune came and asked Paul, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” And he said, “Yes.”
text.Rotherham: And the captain, coming up, said to him—Tell me! Art, thou, a, Roman? And, he, said—Yea!
text.KJV: 22:27 Then the chiefe captaine came; and said vnto him, Tell me, art thou a Romane? He said, Yea.

text.NRSV: The tribune answered, “It cost me a large sum of money to get my citizenship.” Paul said, “But I was born a citizen.”
text.Rotherham: And the captain answered—I, for a large sum, this citizenship acquired! And, Paul, said—But, I, am even, [free-] born!
text.KJV: 22:28 And the chiefe captaine answered, With a great summe obteined I this freedome. And Paul said, But I was free borne.

text.NRSV: Immediately those who were about to examine him drew back from him; and the tribune also was afraid, for he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had bound him.
text.Rotherham: Straightway, therefore, they who were about to put him to the test, withdrew from him; and, even the captain, was struck with fear, when he found out he was, a Roman, and because, him, he had bound.
text.KJV: 22:29 Then straightway they departed from him which should haue examined him: and the chiefe captaine also was afraid after he knew that he was a Romane, & because he had bound him.

text.NRSV: Since he wanted to find out what Paul was being accused of by the Jews, the next day he released him and ordered the chief priests and the entire council to meet. He brought Paul down and had him stand before them.
text.Rotherham: But, on the morrow, being minded to get to know the certainty as to why he was being accused by the Jews, he released him, and ordered the High-priests and all the High-council to come together; and, bringing down Paul, set him before them.
text.KJV: 22:30 On the morrow, because he would haue knowen the certaintie wherefore he was accused of the Iewes, he loosed him from his bands, and commanded the chiefe Priests and all their Councill to appeare, and brought Paul downe, and set him before them.

text.Base: While Paul was looking intently at the council he said, “Brothers, up to this day I have lived my life with a clear conscience before God.”
text.KJV: 23:1 And Paul earnestly beholding the council, said, Men and brethren, I haue liued in all good conscience before God vntill this day.

text.Base: Then the high priest Ananias ordered those standing near him to strike him on the mouth.
text.KJV: 23:2 And the high Priest Ananias commanded them that stood by him, to smite him on the mouth.

text.Base: At this Paul said to him, “God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! Are you sitting there to judge me according to the law, and yet in violation of the law you order me to be struck?”
text.KJV: 23:3 Then saith Paul vnto him, God shall smite thee, thou whited wall: for sittest thou to iudge mee after the Law, and commandest mee to be smitten contrary to the Law?

text.Base: Those standing nearby said, “Do you dare to insult God’s high priest?”
text.KJV: 23:4 And they that stood by, said, Reuilest thou Gods high Priest?

text.Base: And Paul said, “I did not realize, brothers, that he was high priest; for it is written, ‘You shall not speak evil of a leader of your people.’ ”
text.KJV: 23:5 Then said Paul, I wist not, brethren, that hee was the high Prist: For it is written, Thou shalt not speake euill of the ruler of thy people.

text.Base: When Paul noticed that some were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he called out in the council, “Brothers, I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees. I am on trial concerning the hope of the resurrection of the dead.”
text.KJV: 23:6 But when Paul perceiued that the one part were Sadducees, and the other Pharisees, hee cryed out in the Councill, Men and brethren, I am a Pharisee, the sonne of a Pharisee: of the hope and resurrection of the dead, I am called in question.

text.Base: When he said this, a dissension began between the Pharisees and the Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
text.KJV: 23:7 And when hee had so said, there arose a dissension betweene the Pharisees and the Sadducees: and the multitude was diuided.

text.Base: (The Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, or angel, or spirit; but the Pharisees acknowledge all three.)
text.KJV: 23:8 For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, neither Angel, nor spirit: but the Pharisees confesse both.

text.Base: Then a great clamor arose, and certain scribes of the Pharisees’ group stood up and contended, “We find nothing wrong with this man. What if a spirit or an angel has spoken to him?”
text.KJV: 23:9 And there arose a great cry: and the Scribes that were of the Pharisees part arose, and stroue, saying, Wee finde no euill in this man: but if a spirit or an Angel hath spoken to him, let vs not fight against God.

text.Base: When the dissension became violent, the tribune, fearing that they would tear Paul to pieces, ordered the soldiers to go down, take him by force, and bring him into the barracks.
text.KJV: 23:10 And when there arose a great dissension, the chiefe captaine fearing lest Paul should haue bene pulled in pieces of them, commanded the souldiers to goe downe, and to take him by force from among them, and to bring him into the castle.

text.Base: That night the Lord stood by him and said, “Keep up your courage! For just as you have testified for me in Jerusalem, so you must bear witness also in Rome.”
text.KJV: 23:11 And the night folowing, the Lord stood by him, and saide, Bee of good cheere, Paul: for as thou hast testified of mee in Hierusalem, so must thou beare witnesse also at Rome.

text.Base: In the morning the Jews joined in a conspiracy and bound themselves by an oath neither to eat nor drink until they had killed Paul.
text.KJV: 23:12 And when it was day, certaine of the Iewes banded together, and bound themselues vnder a curse, saying, that they would neither eate nor drinke till they had killed Paul.

text.Base: There were more than forty who joined in this conspiracy.
text.KJV: 23:13 And they were more then fourtie which had made this conspiracie.

text.Base: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, “We have strictly bound ourselves by an oath to taste no food until we have killed Paul.
text.KJV: 23:14 And they came to the chiefe Priests and Elders, and said, Wee haue bound our selues vnder a great curse, that wee will eate nothing vntill wee haue slaine Paul.

text.Base: Now then, you and the council must notify the tribune to bring him down to you, on the pretext that you want to make a more thorough examination of his case. And we are ready to do away with him before he arrives.”
text.KJV: 23:15 Now therefore ye with the Councill, signifie to the chiefe captaine that he bring him downe vnto you to morrow, as though yee would enquire something more perfectly concerning him: and we, or euer he come neere, are ready to kill him.

text.Base: Now the son of Paul’s sister heard about the ambush; so he went and gained entrance to the barracks and told Paul.
text.KJV: 23:16 And when Pauls sisters sonne heard of their laying in wait, hee went and entred into the castle, & told Paul.

text.Base: Paul called one of the centurions and said, “Take this young man to the tribune, for he has something to report to him.”
text.KJV: 23:17 Then Paul called one of the Centurions vnto him, and said, Bring this yong man vnto the chiefe captaine: for he hath a certaine thing to tell him.

text.Base: So he took him, brought him to the tribune, and said, “The prisoner Paul called me and asked me to bring this young man to you; he has something to tell you.”
text.KJV: 23:18 So he took him, and brought him to the chiefe captaine, and said, Paul the prisoner called me vnto him, and praied mee to bring this yong man vnto thee, who hath something to say vnto thee.

text.Base: The tribune took him by the hand, drew him aside privately, and asked, “What is it that you have to report to me?”
text.KJV: 23:19 Then the chiefe captaine tooke him by the hand, and went with him aside priuately, and asked him, What is that thou hast to tell me?

text.Base: He answered, “The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though they were going to inquire more thoroughly into his case.
text.KJV: 23:20 And he said, The Iewes haue agreed to desire thee, that thou wouldest bring downe Paul to morrow into the Council, as though they would enquire somewhat of him more perfectly.

text.Base: But do not be persuaded by them, for more than forty of their men are lying in ambush for him. They have bound themselves by an oath neither to eat nor drink until they have killed him. They are ready now and are waiting for your consent.”
